title: Introduction to Systems Thinking for Policymaking
description: When policy problems are complex, the instinct is often to diagnose the cause and apply a direct fix. But in practice, such an approach can produce unintended consequences, or fail entirely, because the real drivers lie elsewhere in the system. Introduction to Systems Thinking for Policymaking is a one-day, hands-on programme designed to strengthen junior officers’ critical thinking in complex policy work. You will move beyond linear problem-solving to adopt a systems perspective – understanding how your policy challenges are embedded within broader systems that shape behaviours and outcomes. Using a practical Adaptive Action approach applied to real cases and guided exercises, you will develop sharper problem framing, learn to identify patterns, map interdependencies, and make more informed policy decisions in complex environments.
learning_outcomes:
Describe what is meant by a system and systems thinking.
Explain the importance of systems thinking in policymaking
who_should_attend: New officers and/or individual contributors in the Policy and Planning function.

title: Partnership and Engagement 301
description:
(Do note that CRPE301 was the previous code for this programme)

Citizen engagement projects are complex on multiple fronts. On the front-end, they involve significant interface between government agencies and citizen stakeholders; on the back-end they involve substantial coordination across departments within agencies, and often between agencies if issues cut across multiple policy domains. They also require simultaneous management of multiple stakeholders: Political Office Holders and senior management of an agency, stakeholders on the issues being discussed, citizen participants and internal staffing teams.

These multiple, overlapping complexities require a particular blend of leadership skills that the programme will introduce – these skills include polarity thinking, complexity science and systems thinking, managing and designing experiences for multiple personality types, and harnessing a range of leadership action logics.

The programme will focus on practical application of th
learning_outcomes:
Appreciate the range of motivations for different stakeholders involved in citizen engagement and partnership projects, both in and outside of government, and at both senior and junior levels within governments
Apply a range of leadership and managerial tools to the specific challenges, opportunities and needs of citizen engagement and partnership projects
Recognise that as senior leaders, they can have significant agency to influence the outcomes and/or success of citizen engagement and partnership projects, even if ultimate authority might rest with more senior officers, and expertise might lie among both more junior staff and external facilitators/partners
Identify how their personal leadership can be manifested in constructive ways for citizen engagement and partnership projects
who_should_attend: Directors and above with significant working experience on citizen engagement at the leadership level and/or are leading multiple complex citizen engagement projects

title: Applied AI in Practice
description:
Turn your everyday tasks into productivity gains with AI

You've learned the basics, now it's time to put that knowledge into practice. Through this half-day programme, you'll learn to integrate government and commercial AI tools - Pair, PlatformAI (Govtech), Orca (MOM), NotebookLM into daily tasks through hands-on application. 

You'll work on authentic work scenarios like analysing data, brainstorming ideas, conducting research, or handling service requests by combining multiple AI platforms.

What you'll gain:

Workflow integration: Learn how AI fits naturally into your workflows and processes
 
Public service case studies: Work on scenarios that align with your role, covering research, presentation, or customer service, etc.
 
Ready-to-use prompts: Leave with practical knowledge and examples you can adapt to your daily tasks

Note: This programme has replaced V11AIH - The AI Handbook: A Beginner's Guide. We'll gradually update the content as new AI tools become available. 

Learner
learning_outcomes:
Apply effective prompting techniques to enhance AI-assisted work  
Integrate and combine AI tools responsibly into public service workflows 
Leverage AI for diverse tasks across multiple domains
who_should_attend:
You are a public officer who is beginning to use AI at work. You know basic prompting skills and have used tools like ChatGPT and Pair for tasks such as drafting emails and summarisation. 

You're looking to learn how to integrate generative AI into your daily work and discover how to combine multiple AI tools effectively.

title: Powering Tomorrow: Inside Shenzhen's Digital Ecosystem
description:
(Do note that CRGISN was the previous code for this programme)

Join us on an immersive journey into China's digital ecosystem and witness its technological advances firsthand. Step into Shenzhen, China's Silicon Valley and innovation powerhouse, to see how it is transforming public service delivery and urban solutions.

Singapore and China's digital partnership has grown significantly through key initiatives including the Chongqing Connectivity Initiative (2015), the Shenzhen Smart Cities Initiative (2019), and the Digital Policy Dialogue (2023). These partnerships have paved the way for meaningful collaboration in digital connectivity, innovation and entrepreneurship, and talent exchange development.

As Singapore continues to deepen its international digital partnerships, this programme offers a unique opportunity for public service officers to explore how China's cutting-edge digital innovations can be contextualised and applied to Singapore’s Smart Nation journey.

This programme
learning_outcomes:
Learn about Shenzhen's smart city vision and how digitalisation is transforming Chinese megacities
Explore Shenzhen's AI innovations and applications in urban solution
Visit leading tech companies to learn about digital innovations and solutions
who_should_attend: Officers should preferably have at least 3–7 years of work experience in digital development and/or international relations-related portfolios, with a strong emphasis on those in or preparing for team lead positions.

title: Municipal Services Delivery Programme
description:
(Do note that CRMSDC1 was the previous code for this programme)

Have you ever wondered about the different roles of our municipal agencies? Or wished you were able to navigate the various protocols and Standard Operating Procedure (SOPs) with ease to help citizens better?

In this programme, you will learn to solve municipal issues with practical resources. You will also get acquainted with fellow officers from other municipal agencies and have the opportunity to discuss complex issues for effective municipal services delivery. We will also share case studies and good practices (e.g. in responding to public feedback).

This programme is developed in partnership with the Municipal Services Office (MSO).

You are required to complete “Delivering Municipal Services 101” in LEARN before coming to the classroom segment.
learning_outcomes:
Recognise the roles and responsibilities of agencies in the municipal sector
Identify tools to resolve inter-agency issues
Learn citizen-centred approaches in responding to public feedback
who_should_attend: You are a frontline officer i.e. Ops managers and officer (MX12 and below) with up to 1 year of experience in managing municipal issues in a OneService agency. Participants must be in a OneService agency i.e. BCA, HDB, LTA, NEA, NParks, PA, PUB, SLA, SPF and URA.

title: Parliamentary Replies And Processes
description:
(Do note that CR-P11PPAR was the previous code for this programme)

As a public officer, you may find yourself having to provide answers to questions posed to your ministry during parliamentary session.

In this programme, you will learn how to draft effective parliamentary replies which emphasises your organisation's key messages to the public. You will also learn the business of Parliament and understand the PQ system, its procedures and timelines.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the PQ system, its procedures and timelines.
Draft replies that address the main concerns of the Members of Parliament (MPs), their constituents and the public, and emphasise the organisations key messages to the public
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who may be required to write responses to parliamentary sessions in the course of your work.

title: High Performing Teams by Design
description:
High Performing Teams by Design is a one-day, practice-based workshop for leaders to improve team effectiveness by diagnosing and shaping the conditions that matter most—so you can exercise judgement on where to focus your leadership effort, using the Six Team Conditions framework as a guide.

Rather than reacting to visible issues (e.g. misalignment, slow decisions, ineffective meetings), the programme helps you diagnose the underlying conditions that shape team performance—and exercise judgement on where to intervene for greatest impact.

Core concepts of the Six Team Conditions framework are introduced through prerequisite e-learning modules (delivered as a separate programme), enabling workshop time to focus on applying the framework to your own team, learning from peers, and translating insights into action
learning_outcomes:
Diagnose team challenges at the level of underlying conditions (not just behaviours)
Assess your team’s current conditions and identify the highest-leverage focus area
Learn how other leaders approach similar team challenges across contexts
Leave with a practical action plan and clear next steps to take with your team
who_should_attend: Deputy Directors and Directors leading teams

title: AI Tech Festival: AIgniting Action
description:
AI Tech Festival is back again, this time, to help you in your AI journey, to turn that spark of intention into action!

Join the AI Tech Festival - Igniting Action this year in person and discover how  AI can be applied meaningfully in your work across the public service. Through plenary session, concurrent breakout session, live demonstrations, and interactive showcases, you will explore real-world government use cases and gain practical insights into how you may adopt AI in your work to enhance productivity, decision-making, and service delivery. Learn more about AI tools through play at our AI Playground too! This event offers you a hands-on opportunity to learn, build networks and contacts and build confidence in applying AI in your day-to-day role! 

Curate your own learning for that day by choosing the breakout session of your choice. More details will be shared with you via email upon confirmation of your registration.
learning_outcomes:
We hope the event will enable participants to:

Sharp minds, Smart tools 
Building mindsets and applying critical thinking to get better outcomes from AI, owning the work, and not outsourcing your judgement
 
Domain Exchange – Across Workflow
Discovering possibilities of adopting AI in various functional domains in Public Service, highlighting the opportunities and challenges of AI adoption
 
AI in Action 
AI-related Hands-on experience, providing participants with the skills and confidence to apply & explore AI solutions in their work.
 
Use AI safely and responsibly
Using AI with a clear understanding of its ethical boundaries, data responsibilities, and risks
who_should_attend: This is only for Singapore Public Officers, who are keen to move beyond awareness of AI and seek to adopt AI in the course of work.

title: PaCE – Public Communications and Engagement Executive Programme
description:
(Do note that MSPCE10 was the previous code for this programme)

The Public Communications and Engagement Executive Programme (PaCE), previously known as the Public Communications and Engagement 101 Programme, is a milestone programme that provides an overview of the key thrusts and challenges of government communications. 


In this programme, you will learn about the good practices of effective government communications in five key areas – Strategic Communications, Media Messaging & Engagement, Marketing & Branding, Research & Analysis, and Engagement. 


You will also learn about the government communications structure, use data to shape communication strategy, get the best out of media engagement, explore news treatment by different media, learn how to manage misinformation and fake news, develop a marketing campaign, adopt a digital first strategy for communication efforts, and strengthen engagement and outreach.
learning_outcomes:
- Gain an overview of the core knowledge and skills in 5 critical areas of communications:
Strategic Communications
Media Messaging and Engagement
Marketing and Branding
Research and Data Analysis
Engagement


- Identify digital platforms, consumption patterns and audience analysis for a digital first strategy.


- Be equipped with practical tips and best practices on effective ground execution to deliver impactful communication results and outcomes.


- Identify central resources available for the implementation of government communication work and services e.g. WOG Framework agreements for procurement of translation services, content creation and research, as well as resources for partnership and engagement.
who_should_attend: Singapore Public Service Officers with less than 3 years of experience and who are involved in Public Communications and Engagement work.

title: Integrating Strategic Communications and Engagement for Impact
description:
Government communications must be impactful to achieve the desired outcomes – build understanding and awareness, gain public trust, and support and influence behaviours. This requires an integrated communications and engagement plan that covers the entire spectrum of competencies, from shaping narrative and key messages, staging the comms roll out on policy announcement, driving sense-making/research/message testing to engaging key stakeholders. This three-day programme is designed to illustrate how the various components of effective public communications come together to support policy roll out and ground implementation. 

You'll gain practical skills through three key ways: 

Case Study Analysis: Examine public communications interventions and value-add upstream for specific government policies and learn from best practices
Expert Insights: Learn directly from experienced practitioners who will share their insights and lessons learnt on effective government communication strategies
learning_outcomes:
Design an integrated communications, partnership and engagement (CPE) strategy to meet policy and communications objectives.
Utilise key data and research to identify and analyse specific audience segments, enabling customised messaging and narratives for each group. 
Develop a targeted stakeholder engagement plan to shape public opinion, manage perceptions and build meaningful stakeholder relationships. 
Identify and address potential opportunities and challenges through risk mitigation strategies, including ground sensing, message testing, and counter narratives. 
Establish key performance indicators to measure the progress and effectiveness of the integrated CPE strategy.
who_should_attend:
Middle Managers in the Comms and Engagement function.

$1,019.15/ participant
Including 9% GST of $84.15

About

title: Engagement Immersion for Leaders (Directors)
description:
(Do note that CREIL30 was the previous code for this programme)

In recent years, the citizen and engagement ecosystem in Singapore has transformed, with a greater diversity of vibrant stakeholders with increased interactions and interdependencies amongst them. The challenges we face as a society are becoming more complex and are often referred to as “wicked problems.” Understanding the diverse needs, collaborating with diverse stakeholders, and leveraging their unique strengths are critical to addressing emerging societal challenges.

This programme is designed to be an immersive leadership development programme to equip Public Service leaders with the necessary appreciation, principles and skills required for effective citizen and stakeholder engagements/partnerships. By leveraging the perspectives of citizens and other stakeholders, leaders can better navigate the complexities of emerging challenges and find solutions to them.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the experiences, needs and perspectives of partners and the community in relation to policy outcomes to strengthen policy design and implementation
Discuss case studies for public policy and engagement in Singapore
Gain clarity on your current leadership/work challenges and how multi-stakeholder engagement can support your teams in solving these challenges
Understand relational thinking and empower your teams to harness positive relationships, mobilise stakeholders, and foster strong alliances necessary for change
Learn skills to build openness and strike win-win engagements
Commit to a personal development plan to embrace and support community engagement experiences
who_should_attend: Directors in a policy development, service delivery or in public partnerships and engagement portfolios and want to gain deeper insights to: • Understand how policy intentions may complement or differ from the citizens’ lived experiences • Listen empathetically to the needs of citizens • Acquire new skills in communication and engagement to co-deliver services with citizens and the people and private sector Please note that EIL for Dir-level and above are for officers who are in MX9 and above roles. If required, please discuss with your supervisor on the suitability of the EIL programmes for your job grade.

title: Engagement Immersion for Leaders (Middle Managers)
description:
(Do note that CREIL20 was the previous code for this programme)

In recent years, the citizen and engagement ecosystem in Singapore has transformed, with a greater diversity of vibrant stakeholders with increased interactions and interdependencies amongst them. The challenges we face as a society are becoming more complex, and are often referred to as “wicked problems.” Understanding the diverse needs, collaborating with diverse stakeholders, and leveraging their unique strengths are critical to addressing emerging societal challenges.

The programme is designed as to be an immersive leadership development programme to equip middle managers with the necessary appreciation, principles and skills required for effective citizen and stakeholder engagements/partnerships. By leveraging the perspectives of citizens and other stakeholders, managers can better navigate the complexities of emerging challenges and find solutions to them.
learning_outcomes:
Gather and understand ground realities and perspectives through field observations and conversations 
Deepen stakeholder engagement and partnership skills such as empathy and active listening 
Leverage ground sensing walks to build relationships & bridge gaps in perceptions
Build greater trust through collaboration
Strengthen consensus-building competencies
Gain clarity on current leadership/work challenges and how public engagement can support resolution of these challenges
Adopt relational thinking to harness positive relationships, mobilise stakeholders and foster strong alliances needed for changes
Commit to a personal development plan to apply ground-sensing and community engagement habits
who_should_attend: Middle managers in a policy-development, service delivery or public partnerships and engagement role who want to gain deeper insights to help them: • Understand how policy intentions may complement or differ from the citizens’ lived experiences • Listen empathetically to the needs of citizens • Acquire new skills in communication and engagement to co-deliver services with citizens and the people and private sector Please note that EIL for MMs is designed for officers who are in the job grade MX11a-10 roles. If required, please discuss with your supervisor on the suitability of the EIL programmes for your job grade.

title: Introduction to Sensemaking and Narrative
description:
(Do note that CRSSE10 was the previous code for this programme)

This is an intermediate-level programme that provides an overview of sensemaking and narrative approaches, and how these approaches can be incorporated into your engagement design and implementation.


You will begin by learning about sensemaking through different frames and the background to narrative theory. This is followed by understanding how these frameworks can be used to guide workshop design, question framing, and other aspects of your engagement.
learning_outcomes:
Learn sensemaking frameworks to support better awareness in planning and implementing engagements
Understand the use of narrative as an engagement technique
Explore how to pair and integrate qualitative and quantitative methods
Learn to build studies from stories, to data points, and to sensemaking frameworks for co-developing insight with stakeholders
who_should_attend: This programme is suitable for officers who want to explore how to frame and design engagements to encourage deliberative sensemaking, and to incorporate more narrative-based approaches.

title: Compensation and Benefits in the Public Service
description:
(Do note that CRCABS was the previous code for this programme)

Dive into the latest practices, beliefs, and principles on how you can review your organisation's total rewards strategy!

The programme offers insights and practical perspectives on fixed and variable compensation, and their linkages to performance management. This is also an opportunity for you to learn how compensation is structured and the varying considerations and approaches to create a rewards strategy linked to organisational and HR strategy.
learning_outcomes:
By the end of this programme, you will be able to:

Create a rewards strategy linked to organisational and HR strategy
Understand the concept of Total rewards, including the use of base pay, variable pay and benefits
Design rewards programmes that take into consideration both internal equity and external competitiveness
Write Job Descriptions and perform Job Analyses and Evaluations
Explain how performance management process and systemis integrated with other HR activities
Create compensation structures and pay ranges as well as annual merit increase strategies
Understand the use of incentives and their role in a rewards strategy
Recognise local conditions in Singapore and new trends in the compensation arena that may affect design of future remuneration strategies.
who_should_attend: You are a HR practitioner doing Compensation and Benefits.

title: Systems Leadership Foundation
description:
In today’s complex landscape, problems are increasingly recognised as “wicked problems”—intricate challenges that defy simple solutions. Addressing these requires a shift away from traditional leadership and approaches to problem-solving towards systemic understanding and collective action.

Organised in collaboration with the SIM Centre for Systems Leadership, this two-part programme is designed to provide Public Officers with the tools and perspectives to navigate and effectively address complex problems. Through an Action Learning Project component, participants will develop a plan for a collaborative change initiative that creates real-world impact for their organisation and communities.

The trainer will be Thomas Lim, Dean of the Centre for Systems Leadership, SIM Academy. https://go.gov.sg/thomaslim
learning_outcomes:
The programme will enable participants to:

Understand and apply the concepts of systems leadership and its core capabilities to navigate complex challenges beyond single-organisation solutions.
Apply systems thinking approaches and tools to analyse wicked, multi-stakeholder problems, moving beyond symptom-fixing towards high-leverage interventions.
Influence and align the broader ecosystem, including partners and communities towards shared outcomes.
Design and develop an actionable change project grounded in their organisation’s  mission through an Action Learning Project.
Lead as a system steward by balancing purpose, impact and sustainability while scaling change responsibly.
Strengthen personal resilience, leadership clarity and confidence to lead complex change with credibility and sustained energy.
who_should_attend:
Deputy Directors, Senior Deputy Directors and Directors

We recommend you attend the programme with at least one of your intact team members (could be junior to you) so that you can work on your organisation’s project together.

If you have questions, please see the FAQ which compares Systems Leadership Foundation with the Basic Systems Thinking course at CSC. https://go.gov.sg/faq-slfoundation

title: [Virtual Classroom] Grow your Career Journey for Support Officers
description:
(Do note that CRGCJVL was the previous code for this programme)

Having witnessed how technology has reinvented jobs, and hybrid work arrangements have affected the way we worked, many of us are aware of the increasingly diverse, disruptive and complex work landscape.  

While we acknowledge that it is vital to take ownership of our careers by being resilient, agile and embracing the spirit of life-long learning through upskilling or re-skilling, how might we go about growing our career? 

In this programme, develop a customised Learning and Career Development Plan and explore developmental opportunities within the Public Service through a SWOT analysis.

You will also be equipped with a growth mindset, learn to manage your emotions and overcome changes and challenges that you may encounter in your career journey.

Lastly, learn how to effectively engage in a career conversation with your supervisor and have a one-to-one career session to discuss more on how you can navigate your caree
learning_outcomes:
Understand the future world of work and the impact and impending changes to your job roles, learning and career development.
Uncover your current reality (Strengths, Weaknesses and Threats) and discover possible career development opportunities through SWOT analysis.
Explore opportunities for learning and career development using public sector resources.
Discuss how you can have an effective career conversation with your supervisors using the GROW model.
Design and implement a personal action plan for your career development.
who_should_attend: You are a support officer in the Singapore Public Service who wants to take ownership of your career and have a better understanding of how to do so.

title: Bridges
description:
(Do note that MSB was the previous code for this programme)

As you step into your role as a middle manager, you may experience multiple changes during this transition phase. You may find yourself grappling with your leadership identity, questioning your value-add while navigating new responsibilities and unfamiliar challenges. Bridges is designed to be your support structure through this pivotal phase, providing a space to explore these challenges alongside peers on a similar journey. Through this programme, you will build a solid foundation for your leadership style, develop practical skills to lead, deliver and influence effectively, and form strong connections with fellow middle managers.
learning_outcomes:
Deepen awareness about your leadership identity and impact so as to leverage strengths and act on growth areas
Gain practical skills and insights to influence culture, develop capacity and capability so that your team(s) and organisation can perform effectively and sustainably
Develop strategies to engage stakeholders within and outside the Public Service so as to understand diverse perspectives and deliver on shared outcomes 
Explain the considerations behind government priorities so that you can translate intent into implementation 
Form networks with fellow middle managers to support complex work
who_should_attend: You are a newly-appointed Middle Manager within your first 2 years of assuming a middle management role and has prior experience leading teams (i.e., you are not new manager).

title: [Virtual Classroom] Engaging Customers Professionally on Phone and Email
description:
(Do note that CRTSS12 was the previous code for this programme)

Love it or hate it, customers will continue to use the telephone as a means of communication to handle complex queries even in the face of digital transformation. It is therefore important that we learn effective telephone techniques, and excel in them, so that we our effectiveness, efficiency and professionalism can be experienced by the customers. 

This will not only enhance your image as a professional in your job, but also the image of your organisation. The programme will also impart basic email skills for when you are required to follow up your telephone conversation in writing.
learning_outcomes:
Recap the CARE Service Principles in the Public Service and its application in telephone interactions
Articulate the importance of attitude and how to manage your attitude to stay positive and respond calmly in stressful customer situations
Explain the 4 phases of telephone interactions and the service standards in the Public Service
Apply the critical core skills needed in telephone interactions
Apply the approach, and techniques to handle commonly encountered difficult customer situations
Use the email guidelines and tips when following up over email with customers after the telephone interaction
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who interact with customers on the phone.

title: Advanced Data Visualisation with Tableau
description: Move beyond basic charts and create powerful visualisations that match your analytical needs. Learn to create animated scatter plots that reveal trends over time, Gantt charts for project tracking, and Pareto charts for impact analysis. You'll also master how to prepare your data for Tableau by wrangling, combining, and transforming complex raw datasets into visualisation-ready formats. Gain advanced techniques to analyse data more effectively, craft stories that drive decisions, and turn complex information into clear, compelling visuals. Note: This is an intermediate - advanced programme. If you're new to Tableau, you're encouraged to attend Data Visualisation for Effective Communications first before attending this programme.  If you're unsure, please use this self-assessment check list to check your suitability.
learning_outcomes: Apply key design principles to ensure that your visualisations convey the right message. Design complex multi-layered visualisations using advanced chart types  Clean, transform, and prepare messy datasets using Power query and Tableau Create relationships between data tables and develop data models for analysis. Use calculated fields, table calculations, and advanced filtering to derive new insights Build interactive dashboards that answer key business questions and support strategic decision-making

title: Futurecraft 201:  Scenario Planning Workshop
description: ( Do note that CRFC201 was the previous code for this programme ) Scenarios show what about the future we cannot change, and what we can change. That is the value of scenarios: sketching for us the terrain within which we have room to manoeuvre. Scenarios are a complement to quantitative models to forecast the future because they analyse qualitative factors that drive change, such as shifts in values and interests, which are not easily captured by quantitative models. Scenarios can spark “strategic conversations” on possible futures, and allow the discussion of questions that may be otherwise uncomfortable to discuss. This two-day programme is intended for agencies planning to undertake a full-scale scenario project. You will be taught the full methodology of scenario planning, which builds on the basics from FutureCraft 101.
learning_outcomes: Define the focal question Identify and analyse driving forces Construct scenario frameworks Articulate and communicate the scenario stories Translate scenarios to strategies. This workshop provides a much more in-depth and hands-on experience of scenario planning than FutureCraft 101, though this programme builds upon the basics taught in that course. You are encouraged to attend FutureCraft 101 before attending FutureCraft 201.

title: Introduction to Policy Design and Evaluation Tools
description:
(Do note that CRPDE10 was the previous code for this programme)

What tools can public officers use to design and evaluate new policies and programmes? Are these tools mutually exclusive?

In this practitioner-oriented programme, you will learn how to use tools such as Behavioural Insights, Data Analytics and Cost-Benefit Analysis to design and evaluate policies and programmes. Through examples and case study discussions, you will gain a basic understanding of how these tools can be used at different stages of the policy process as well as ways to address the limitations of these tools.
learning_outcomes:
Describe how the policy tools can be used to design and evaluate policies and programmes;
Explain how the different policy tools are used at different stages of the policy development and evaluation process.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer (Senior Executive – Senior Manager level) involved in policy design, formulation, analysis and evaluation and/or interested to find out more about policy design and evaluation.

title: [Virtual Classroom] AI-yoh! Is this AI right?
description:
Prompting is just the start. In the age of AI, the real skill lies in evaluating what it gives you. This webinar is all about sharpening your critical thinking skills when working with AI. You’ll learn how to spot red flags, challenge assumptions, and refine prompts to get outputs that are not just impressive, but also accurate and reliable. 

 

Please note that the class will be held on Zoom, and the link will be sent to you via Postman. Please keep a lookout on your emails!

You would need your own COMET/GSIB laptops to access GovAI tools for this programme.
learning_outcomes:
Apply evaluation techniques and critical thinking approaches to identify errors, gaps, and biases in AI-generated content. 

Refine your prompts with frameworks to craft clearer, more targeted prompts that get you responses you can actually use. 

Apply simple, practical fact-checking methods to verify AI-generated output, so you can present, report, and recommend with confidence.
who_should_attend:
Public officers who want to sharpen their critical thinking and prompt refinement skills to work more effectively and confidently with AI.

 

Please note that this is a re-run of the webinar series in Sept-Oct 2025.

title: Selection Interview - Competency Based Skills & Techniques
description:
(This is the physical modality of what was previously titled [Virtual Classroom] Selection Interview - Competency Based Skills & Techniques (CR-V11SCB))

Increasingly, organisations seek talent who can excel in today's roles whilst demonstrating the potential to thrive as the operating context evolves. As a manager, how do you identify candidates who possess both immediate capability and future growth potential to meet your organisation's objectives?

This workshop equips you with systematic competency-based interviewing techniques to evaluate candidates against the critical competencies and qualities essential for specific roles. You'll learn structured approaches that move beyond intuition-based to competency-driven selection of candidates who will thrive in their roles and contribute to your organisation's long-term success.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the fundamentals of an end-to-end Recruitment and Selection process
Uphold an objective and professional approach of the Recruitment and Selection process 
Establish the business need for a required position and the desired critical competencies and qualities
Discover best practices in selecting the best fit candidates 
Apply competency-based interviewing techniques to assess competencies and qualities of potential candidates
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who manages the recruitment and slecction process in your agency and/or conducts selection interviews.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Design Play!
description:
(Do note that CRDEPVL was the previous code for this programme)

Ever wondered about the importance of Design Thinking and how to apply it in the workplace?

As public officers, it is important to challenge long-held assumptions and empathise with stakeholders and citizens to uncover and address their needs in public sector challenges. We must also learn to experiment with ideas to improve work processes and service delivery, and be open to new approaches.

Sign up for this introductory programme now to learn more about human-centred design and approach, framed by the 5Es and Public Service Innovation Framework, and how it can be relevant to your work.
learning_outcomes:
Describe the design thinking process, with a focus on public sector application 
Recognise the importance of empathy with users and understanding people's needs in public sector challenges 
Discover the concept and value of iteration in the design thinking process
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is interested in understanding the basics of human-centred design methodologies.

title: The Six Levels of Service by Ron Kaufman
description:
(Do note that ELSLS13 was the previous code for this programme)

In this programme, you will learn the fundamental service principles to upgrade service levels and uplift your customer’s experience. You will learn how to delight your customers, support your colleagues and help build an uplifting service culture.You will find new ways to improve your service and create more service value for your customers and colleagues.
learning_outcomes:
Recognise different levels of internal and external service
Understand why service must continuously improve
Identify specific action steps to upgrade your service
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is keen in Service Excellence.

title: RPA for Finance Officers
description:
(Do note that CRRPAFI was the previous code for this programme)

Finance processes can come in large volumes, and often they are repetitive, tedious, and cumbersome. With Robotic Process Automation (RPA), spending the bulk of your office hours working on tons of mundane finance processes can be a thing of the past. While the term RPA may sound intimidating to non-tech officers, there is an increasing number of tools, such as the UiPath StudioX, that allow business users to easily adopt RPA for routine tasks.

In this one-day programme, we will introduce you to the basics of RPA, explore suitable finance processes for automation and discuss some best practices for the successful implementation of RPA. You will also have the opportunity to roll up your sleeves and work on some finance use cases to enhance your learning.
learning_outcomes:
By the end of this programme, you will be able to gain insights on how an RPA software (UiPath Studio X) works, and be comfortable in experimenting with the software's functionalities based on identified finance processes or existing used cases. 
Gain conceptual insights on RPA; how it works, its key features, functions, capabilities, risks, and challenges.
Identify suitable processes for automation within the finance function.
Assess the suitability and feasibility of the identified finance processes for RPA to be implemented.
Describe the end-to-end RPA implementation approach; including the activities, roles and key outputs required for a successful implementation.

Do note that this 1-day course is intended to provide you with an understanding of how RPA can be applied and help you to automate your work and what are the key criteria for a process or task to be a good candidate for you to automate. While there will be hands-on examples to help you understand  RPA and further elabora
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Finance officer and are interested to learn more about how you can adopt RPA for your finance tasks.

title: Predictive Analytics Made Easy (with Excel)
description:
(Do note that CRPAME1 was the previous code for this programme)

Would you like to generate relevant and useful insights from existing data so that you can better predict and support decision making at your workplace?

When harnessed right, Excel would allow you to do that and more. Sign up for this programme today and you will learn not only how to apply fundamental predictive analysis techniques to data using tools that already exist in Excel, you will also learn techniques like time series and advanced forecasting, and linear and logistic regression.
learning_outcomes:
Demonstrate the ability to forecast time series using different methods like moving averages and exponential smoothing
Describe advanced forecasting techniques (e.g. Holt Winters model) for time series with seasonality and trend
Apply and make predictions (of numbers) using the regression tool in Excel
Explain how logistic regression can be used to predict classes (or categories)
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is an individual contributor and interested in doing predictive analytics work with Excel.

title: Human Resource Leadership Programme (HRLP)
description:
(Do note that MSHLP10 was the previous code for this programme)

 

With the rapidly changing global and local environment, the Singapore Public Service needs to transform itself to keep up with these changes. HR plays a critical role in supporting and enabling Public Sector Transformation efforts.

Under the dynamic Public Service’s operating environment, HR needs to play an active role part in developing a future-ready workforce, partnering Senior Management in building a strong organisation leadership and enhancing engagement with officers.
learning_outcomes:
Strengthen whole-of-government HR perspectives
Deepen the network of peer support and inculcate a practice of connecting with other HR practitioners for both work and ongoing development
Attain IHRP Certification
Recognise emerging changes in the external landscape, their impact on the Public Service, and implications on the role of HR leaders
Hone skills to become a more effective strategic business partner to organisation leaders in decision making
Examine and leverage on private sector best practices to push for innovation in HR practices
Reflect upon current HR practices and philosophy, and re-examine fundamental assumptions of HR in the Public Service
Build greater awareness of one’s personal leadership style and gaps, and adjust one’s practice of HR leadership to engage stakeholders effectively ​
who_should_attend: You are a Human Resource Director or Deputy Director in your organisation.

title: EQ In Customer Service For Frontline Staff
description:
(Do note that CREQF23 was the previous code for this programme)

Emotional Intelligence (EQ) is an important tool in today's challenging and demanding customer service environment, especially if you encounter many emotionally-charged interactions, be it with internal or external customers.

EQ includes a set of learnable skills involving self-awareness, empathy, optimism, self-direction and motivation to manage customers and inter-personal relationships. Through this course, you will extend your EQ to manage and excel in customer service. You will also obtain new insights into successful people and relationships when learning about EQ and how to translate this knowledge to harness your strengths.
learning_outcomes:
Define emotional intelligence and its importance in customer service.
Develop personal emotional intelligence skills to increase self-awareness, self-management and self-motivation.
Cultivate collaborative relationship to build a supportive work environment.
Enhance empathy to establish emotional connection with customers as well as use assertive skills to have effective communication with them.
Identify the behaviours of challenging customers and ways to handle them.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer working in a frontline role.

title: Crucial Conversations For People Managers (Signature Programme)
description:
(Do note that CRCCM10 was the previous code for this programme)

How do you hold difficult or uncomfortable conversations with your direct reports, peers or reporting officers - especially when issues are sensitive, opinions vary and emotions run high?

Backed by thirty years of social science research, the award-winning Crucial Conversations® programme provides skills to create alignment and agreement on difficult and sensitive topics through open dialogue. This will encourage free and honest communication in your team while maintaining psychological safety and preserving relationships. The result: more cohesive and agile teams and a dynamic and resilient organisation.
learning_outcomes:
Understand how to approach sensitive matters such as performance or attitudinal issues successfully as a people manager
Display tact and sincerity when communicating delicate topics to individuals
Build acceptance within a team so action can be taken to achieve results
Effectively escalate delicate topics to reporting officers such that opinions and suggestions can be heard and considered
who_should_attend: You are a supervisor in the Singapore Public Service with direct reports.

title: Bounce with Service Recovery by Ron Kaufman
description:
(Do note that ELBSR13 was the previous code for this programme)

In this course, you will learn that how you react in a service recovery situation can affect customer loyalty. You will also learn to anticipate service recovery situations and set up systems in advance to ensure a smooth recovery.
You will find new ways to improve your service and create more service value for your customers and colleagues.
learning_outcomes:
Understand why upset customers can be a tragedy or an opportunity
Understand how your attitudes and actions will make the difference
Recognise the three actions you must take to earn higher levels of loyalty in service recovery situations
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is keen on Service Excellence.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Taking Ownership of My Performance at Work using Our Core Competencies
description:
(Do note that CRMCWV was the previous code for this programme)

Our Core Competencies is a set of core competencies that public officers need to develop in order to deliver excellence today and build the Public Service of tomorrow. This programme will equip officers with the necessary knowledge, skills and abilities to manage their own performance and demonstrate the refreshed set of core competencies effectively.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the underlying philosophy and principles of Performance Management in the Civil Service
Uncover personal strengths in achieving performance goals and demonstrating Our Core Competencies
Engage supervisors and take ownership of your own development
Practise effective ways of asking and receiving feedback on your performance
Develop an action plan to manage your performance
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is an individual contributor in your team. Officers with supervisory duties, please attend Managing Staff Performance using Our Core Competencies for Supervisors (CRMCSV) instead.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Introduction to Web Analytics
description:
(Do note that CRWOGVL was the previous code for this programme)

With the rapid growth of digitalisation in our society today, we need to stay in touch with the constant changes in user needs and expectations in order to continue delivering good service. This programme will help you to understand the basics of web analytics and provide you the tools and skills to retrieve and make informed decisions with the insights derived to optimise user experience.
learning_outcomes:
Articulate the basics of what can be tracked using web analytics
Scope a problem statement and design a mechanism to keep track of success
Track and optimise customer experience across digital touchpoints
List and understand the services available in Whole-of-Government Application Analytics (WOGAA)
Use WOGAA as one of the tools to evaluate the health of your services
who_should_attend: You are a Service Practitioner with little / no prior experiences to web analytics, looking to learn more about the relevant framework and web analytics platforms available, and using WOGAA as a tool.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Effective Performance Development Strategies for HR Practitioners
description:
(Do note that CREPDV was the previous code for this programme)

The launch of Our Core Competencies (OCC) serves as a nudge for HR to fundamentally rethink the purpose of Performance Management – beyond mid- and year-end evaluations. It is necessary that HR builds an eco-system that is supportive of, and would strongly encourage, officers to effectively develop and demonstrate the relevant competencies in their roles. 




This programme will equip HR practitioners with the necessary knowledge, skills and abilities to develop strategies in facilitating an organisation’s shift towards a development-oriented and competency-driven performance management approach, also known as, Performance Development.
learning_outcomes:
Explain the needs and benefits of shifting to a performance development framework
Understand HR’s role as a performance development architect and ambassador
Apply key performance development tools and techniques
Design a performance development approach suited to your workplace
who_should_attend: HR Practitioners involved in Performance Management (Middle Management and Above)

title: Executive Certification for OD Professionals
description:
(Do note that MSCODP was the previous code for this programme)

The Executive Certification for OD Professionals (CODP) certification is specially crafted for advanced-level Organisation Development (OD) practitioners in the Singapore Public Service. The learning experience not only draws from a wide range of OD disciplines but also emphasises deep skilling leading to personal and systemic shifts. Our goal for CODP is to prepare practitioners with strong cognitive abilities and concrete skills essential in today’s dynamic and evolving world. With a global perspective on organisational effectiveness and health, we provide a unique approach that allows practitioners to not only handle imminent challenges but also lead their organisations to greater heights.
learning_outcomes:
Immerse in a wide range of Organisation Development disciplines to develop a comprehensive understanding on organisational effectiveness and health
Acquire concrete skills to handle imminent challenges and lead organisations to greater heights
Gain deep insights into working with others to achieve organisational outcomes and integrate workplace learning to become a highly effective Organisation Development practitioner
Note: Each Masterclass offers its own set of learning outcomes, please refer to the respective Masterclasses for more information.
who_should_attend: You are an advanced-level Organisation Development practitioner with a strong passion in OD and a strong motivation to serve the Singapore Public Service and would like to develop your competence and confidence to support your leaders in achieving strategic outcomes and enhancing organisational health. The pre-requisites are generally as follows: 1) OD Practitioner Foundation Programme 2) Graduate Certificate in Organisation Development OR Intermediate OD Practitioner Programme (previous programme title) OR OD Practitioner Certification Programme OR Graduate Diploma in Leading and Sustaining Change (LSC) To provide practitioners with more diverse perspectives and rich learning experiences, this certification is also made available to leaders and/or practitioners outside of the Singapore Public Service, i.e., from non-profit organisations and private sectors, subject to a maximum quota of estimated 20% of in-attendance participants.

title: Deep Dive Diagnostics Workshop for EES
description:
(Do note that CREES02 was the previous code for this programme)

To equip EES reps with an understanding of qualitative research methods to dive deeper into findings.
learning_outcomes: Design and analysis of Semi-Structured Interviews (SSIs) and Focus Group Discussions (FGDs)

title: Take Personal Responsibility by Ron Kaufman
description:
In this course, you will recognise three common human behaviours that exist “below the line”,  and appreciate the value of stepping “above the line” to improve your collaboration with colleagues, your service to customers, and your overall working life. 

where and when you deliver your service and discover how customers perceive and evaluate your service, and where you can improve your service.

You will find new ways to improve your service and create more service value for your customers and colleagues.
learning_outcomes:
Recognise different human behaviours in various service situations.
Understand why Blame, Shame, and Excuses do not result in service improvements. Appreciate the value of “stepping above the line” to Take Personal Responsibility – doing what you can, when you can, to create better service outcomes for your customers and your colleagues.
who_should_attend:
You are a Singapore Public Officer who is keen in Service Excellence.

About

title: Partnership and Engagement 101
description:
(Do note that CRPE101 was the previous code for this programme)

Partnership and Engagement (PE) 101 is a foundation programme designed to develop officers’ instincts and capabilities to engage, partner and empower stakeholders. Officers will build up common PE competencies by learning the fundamentals of sensing and identifying PE opportunities, conducting fruitful engagement sessions, establishing and cultivating relationships, as well as understanding and analysing data and insights.

Officers will also develop new skills and greater self-awareness to enhance the ways they engage and partner stakeholders meaningfully towards shared outcomes. At the end of the course, officers will be invited to join the PE community of practice to collaborate, share information and further hone their skills with PE practitioners.
learning_outcomes:
Exercise good judgement in identifying engagement opportunities and relevant stakeholders. 
Communicate engagement outcomes and expectations clearly to stakeholders. 
Establish and maintain meaningful relationships with stakeholders. 
Analyse the impact of engagement. 
Demonstrate openness, empathy and authenticity in pursuing partnerships and engagements.
who_should_attend:
Officers involved in outreach, partnering and engaging stakeholders and citizens or officers who are embarking on partnership and engagement (PE) efforts. Suitable for officers with less than 2 years of relevant work experience and have not attended prior PE training (e.g. "Public And Stakeholder Engagement Workshop – From Planning To Execution").

Trainers
Shroff Anuradha
Anu is a corporate facilitator and certified organisational coach, with more than 10 years of experience working with senior public sector executives and private sector leaders.

Her areas of expertise are facilitation in strategy, leadership and public policy programmes; and coaching of executives who aspire to take the next step in their career and life. She also offers corporate retreats for organisations who want to take a holistic view of well-being as a strategy for employee engagement. 

Her small group facilitation style is informed by her experience in advanced Gestalt group dynamics which focuses on how gro

title: Navigating Complexity using Advanced Facilitation Tools
description:
(Do note that CRCCF10 was the previous code for this programme)

Complex facilitation methods and processes are designed to align with the nature and challenges of complex systems, and to highlight the importance of sensemaking (over traditional analytical skills). They are focused on creating and sustaining environments where the social construction of a shared understanding of complex issues can emerge. 

Through discussions and sharing, participants can discover the appropriate actions to take, based on the Cynefin® (pronounced kuh-nev-in) domains and their corresponding constraints, to deal with issues which are complex, as opposed to ordered. Participants can also explore question design and learn how the framing of questions can draw out desired responses and discussions. 

This programme introduces the Cynefin® framework and the principles of complex systems, for the selection of appropriate facilitation tools, to better navigate complex topics and situations when engaging citiz
learning_outcomes:
By the end of the course, participants should be able to:

Recognise anthro-complexity, or complexity as it is experienced in human systems, and why complex problems require a different approach to facilitation.
Develop an applied understanding of the Cynefin® framework, and the importance of “bounded applicability” (constraints) to identify the right approaches to dealing with issues which are complex, as opposed to ordered.
Differentiate between Traditional and Complex facilitation, and the distinct facilitator mandates, roles and approaches for each.
Apply principles of complexity to design sessions that are complexity-fit.
Identify a complex facilitation need and build on your understanding of complex systems principles to select the appropriate facilitation approach, structure and roles.
Experiment with creating powerful prompting questions that act as catalysts for rich sensemaking and engagement
Examine why and how the design fundamentals like context, multiple perspectives, and
who_should_attend: This programme is suitable for officers who have experience in facilitating public engagement, and who wish to learn how facilitation can be designed for dealing with complex issues.

title: Facilitation For Public Engagement
description:
(Do note that CRFEN10 was the previous code for this programme)

With a stronger focus on public engagement across the public service, public engagement officers like you can benefit from a deeper awareness and application of facilitation theory and practice in your work. Engaging the Public requires a different skillset than engaging internal stakeholders. This highly experiential and interactive programme will deepen your skills in facilitating public engagement, and its practicum component will give you a valuable opportunity to facilitate and receive developmental feedback from your peers and course facilitators, all in a supportive environment.
learning_outcomes:
Describe the role & responsibilities of a facilitator in public engagement.
Demonstrate a neutral facilitator stance when they facilitate engagement sessions.
Conduct focused discussions based on a pre-designed format.
Design questions that are closely aligned to session objectives and follow-up questions to elicit deeper responses and clarify assumptions.
Conduct effective openings & closings to create a safe and relaxed climate for participants.
Anticipate challenging participant behaviours and use appropriate verbal and non-verbal strategies to manage participants’ behaviours.
Use the divergent and convergent framework in their facilitation practices.
Identify the hallmarks of consensus-building.
who_should_attend: You are a public communication and/or engagement practitioner involved or will be involved in public engagement work. This programme is open to Singapore Public Officers only.

title: L&D Practitioners Programme: From Learning to Performance
description:
(Do note that CRFLND was the previous code for this programme. In FY25, this programme was titled ‘Foundational Learning and Development: From Theory to Performance’.)

As workplace demands evolve, traditional training alone is no longer enough to build the capabilities organisations need. ‘L&D Practitioners Programme: From Learning to Performance’ offers another approach—one that connects learning directly to on-the-job performance and real workplace challenges.

This practical programme introduces participants to performance-led L&D, going beyond content delivery to focus on how learning happens through experience and social interaction. Guided by the 70-20-10 framework, you’ll learn how to design holistic learning experiences that support skill application and behaviour change.
learning_outcomes:
At the end of the programme, you should be able to:

Describe how L&D can contribute to organisational outcomes by addressing performance needs at the workplace
Describe how a holistic approach to learning encompasses formal learning, social and experiential interventions
Design a holistic L&D intervention aligned to a business outcome or capability gap in their organisation.
Outline

Day 1 – Exploring our role in L&D: 

L&D principles to support workplace performance 
Identifying performance needs

Day 2 - Designing a holistic intervention to close performance gaps: 

Ways to enable workplace learning 
Factors that influence how learning is transferred to the workplace
Designing a holistic learning solution that considers on-the-job, social and formal learning interventions
$964.65/ participant
Including 9% GST of $79.65

title: Game Design for Systems Thinking
description:
(Do note that CRSGD02 was the previous code for this programme)

How can games help us navigate complex problems?

We create models to help us understand complex, interconnected systems and plan for emergencies and hypothetical scenarios. Contained within every game is a model of the system it pertains to, connecting component parts, feedback loops and thresholds. Creating games gives us system models we can interact with, which can help us better imagine underlying systems and experiment with hypothetical interventions.

In this 2-day programme, we will look at key principles of game design and interactivity, and how these principles can be applied to making models of evolving, complex issues. We will also be working hands-on to prototype some of these game systems.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the key principles of systems thinking and game design. 
Analyse a real-world system to identify its key components, relationships, and feedback loops, and create a visual systems map to represent them. 
Apply game design principles to transform a systems map into an interactive game prototype that simulates the system's behaviour. 
Evaluate and select appropriate tools and techniques for implementing and communicating systems thinking and game design principles effectively within the Public Service context.
who_should_attend: You should attend if you are interested in, or are currently exploring, using games to model complex systems. This programme is open to Singapore Public Officers only.

title: Building Your Personal Resilience For Support Officers
description:
(Do note that CRBPR23 was the previous code for this programme)

In today's rapidly changing environment and workplace, being resilient has become increasingly important as we face greater demands and challenges. Simply put, resilience is the ability to cope with stress, overcome diversity or adapt positively to change and it is a process that involves our behaviour, thoughts and actions.

Learn how you can develop and enhance your resilience to gain greater well-being on both professional and personal levels through resilience techniques and frameworks that will be shared in this programme.
learning_outcomes:
Define the concept of resilience and its significance in sustaining positive relationships at work and in life.
Acquire practical techniques and frameworks for building resilience to effectively manage change, crisis, and difficult emotions.
Apply resiliency skills and techniques to achieve a state of calm focus when facing difficult customer situations.
Examine the relationship between resilience and well-being, and how enhancing resilience can contribute to greater overall wellness.
Identify personal resilience strengths and areas for development through self-assessment and reflection.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is keen to develop stronger personal resilience to better support your team and department.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Procurement 101 for Public Officers
description:
(Do note that CRPEPVL was the previous code for this programme)

How has procurement evolved in the Singapore Public Service? What are the new procurement methods available?

This programme will reinforce your basic understanding of public sector procurement by introducing innovative procurement methods, important skillsets and knowledge, while maintaining your basic awareness of prudence and compliance.
learning_outcomes:
Describe the major Government Procurement Rules and Guidelines 
Describe the direction of Innovation in Government Procurement 
Explain the common forms of bid rigging and how to minimise its risks 
Explain the intricacies of Specifications and Evaluation 
Describe practical aspects of Stakeholder Management 
Describe practical aspects of Supplier Management and linking it back to the different aspects of the procurement lifecycle 
Apply practical aspects of Contract Management to avoid audit issues and improve management of suppliers
who_should_attend:
You are a non-procurement public officer who performs procurement work as part of your role. You are recommended to complete Procurement e-Learning 1.1* (DLP-E11PEL), before attending this programme.

*Please note that Procurement e-Learning 1.1 was previously known as basic procurement training (MPFM1, PNP10) or the e-learning pathways (ELPELP1, ELPELP2 or ELPELP3).

title: CFFP x Explore My Career through Conversations
description:
*Prerequisite Note: This workshop is available as an in-house programme and must be delivered as Part 2 of the CFFP curriculum to create a seamless, integrated 1-day learning experience.

Most of the successful careers are built through intentional dialogue with key stakeholders. This is a practical, action-oriented workshop designed to help you take charge of your professional trajectory.

You will learn to map your professional network, adapt the structured GROW framework for different stakeholders, and actively practice navigating career conversation challenges. 

You won't just learn why these conversations matter—you will walk away with a personalized script and a concrete action plan to execute them with confidence.
learning_outcomes:
Understand how conversations with key stakeholders (e.g. supervisors, peers, mentors, coaches) can support career development and progression.
Identify the different forms of guidance and opportunities that supervisors, peers, mentors, and coaches can provide.
Use GROW framework to prepare and practise seeking feedback, guidance, and opportunities with key stakeholders.
Develop an action plan to engage key stakeholders and move forward with greater clarity and confidence.
who_should_attend:
This programme is specifically designed for public sector officers who are:

In the 'Explore' Career Life Stage: Looking to adapt to a new role, gather feedback, or discover potential pathways for growth.
Proactive Professionals: Anyone wanting to build stronger, more strategic partnerships with supervisors, mentors, coaches, and peers to unlock hidden professional development opportunities.

title: Supervisor's Guide to Careerfitness Conversations
description:
(Do note that CRCDM10 was the previous code for this programme)

As a supervisor, you play a vital role in helping your officers manage and develop their careers. In this programme, you will gain practical knowledge and skills to guide your officers in achieving their career aspirations while also aligning with organisational goals. You will also receive a 1 hour one-on-one coaching session with a career coach to strengthen your ability to manage your officers' career development.
learning_outcomes:
Apply effective strategies to support officers’ career growth by identifying development opportunities and providing constructive feedback
Guide your officers in crafting a career development plan using Our Core Competencies (OCCs) as a framework, integrating individual growth with organisation priorities and team objectives
Enhance quality of career conversations by recognising personal blind spots and motivations to provide more balanced and objective guidance
who_should_attend: You are a supervisor/reporting officer in the Singapore Public Service managing one or more officers.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Introduction to Behavioural Insights
description:
(Do note that CREPD11 was the previous code for this programme)

How can we design interventions that effectively shape public perceptions and behaviours? Public service officers are becoming increasingly aware of the importance of behavioural insights for effective and evidence-based intervention design. Through a series of lectures, you will gain a comprehensive introduction to the key ideas in behavioural insights and be equipped with the foundation for designing and implementing policies in a behaviourally compatible way.
learning_outcomes:
Identify common biases and heuristics that affect the public and policymakers.
 
Explain the key concepts and applications of behavioural insights to design effective policies.
 
Describe the benefits and limitations of approaches that draw on behavioral insights.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer involved in areas that have a strong element of behavioural change on the part of citizens or customers.

title: ComplianceConnect: Designing Better Regulatory Experience
description:
In this ComplianceConnect programme, discover how leading regulatory agencies are redesigning regulatory experiences through citizen and business-centric approaches. Learn from successful local and overseas case studies, whilst exploring principles for designing better regulatory experience.

Through experiential activities, collaborative redesign workshops, and cross-functional discussions, participants will develop practical skills to identify regulatory pain-points and design integrated, stakeholder-centric regimes that leverage technology and cross-agency collaboration. 

Join fellow practitioners and gain actionable insights to lead your agency's shift towards regulatory excellence that builds public trust whilst reducing compliance costs for citizens and businesses.
learning_outcomes:
By the end of the session, participants will be able to:

Apply stakeholder-centric approaches to design regulatory solutions that balance stakeholder (e.g. business) needs with regulatory outcomes.
Develop risk-based compliance strategies through co-creation with stakeholders.
Integrate technologies and adaptive approaches to streamline processes and enhance regulatory responsiveness.
who_should_attend: This programme is open to all Regulatory, Compliance and Enforcement officers.

title: Every Door Is The Right Door (NWD Simulation Workshop)
description:
(Do note that CREDR13 was the previous code for this programme)

Members of the public generally view the Public Service as a single entity. Since its inception in 2004, the No Wrong Door policy has been largely successful in helping our public officers deal with misdirected feedback or cross-agency issues brought up by the public. But what happens when the issues are not so straightforward? Or there seems to be "no right door"?

 

This workshop is designed to help public officers in frontline teams better understand and operationalise the No Wrong Door (NWD) policy and the First Responder Protocol (FRP).

 

The workshop will have a mix of frontline officers and supervisors from different agencies attending it together to learn about the NWD/FRP steps and accompanying skill sets through case simulations in a classroom setting. Going beyond just a "tell" session, this workshop simulates real-life challenges and dilemmas that frontline officers and their supervisors face in the course
learning_outcomes:
Provide inter-agency customer service according to the procedures and standards specified in the NWD policy and the FRP
 
Display a WOG attitude and the First Responder spirit when responding to customer requests that involve multiple agencies
 
Demonstrate customer service skills and people skills in email, face-to-face and phone interactions with customers and colleagues from other agencies when responding to inter-agency customer requests
who_should_attend:
You are a Singapore Public Officer whose work involves inter-agency coordination as you deliver service to the public.

Trainers
Hu See Hwa (Pamela/Pam)
Pam has more than 35 years of experience in the private and public sectors. Over the last 10 years, she was actively involved in education and training, facilitation and consultancy in customer service, data protection, human resource and change management, corporate communications, and business process improvements in the Infocom, media, and the government sector. She also developed various courseware in the areas of customer service, mystery shopping, and government IT services.

Pam was also a National Business Excellence Assessor with SPRING’s Business Excellence Certification & Awards Assessment Team. She had built up assessment and audit competencies in the government, retail, education, and construction sectors assessing for the People Excellence Award, Innovation Excellence Award, and Service Medallion Award.

Pam served as

title: Multipliers for Leading Transformation
description:
Next run: 23 July 2026

Please register for this programme here - Registration form for Multipliers for Leading Transformation on 23 July 2026 | FormSG

Course fees are at $895 (inclusive of $80.55 GST) before 40% subsidy for public service officers.

(Do note that CRMLTX was the previous code for this programme)

You're facing increasing pressure to act swiftly, maintain relevance, and stay ahead of the curve in today's rapidly changing landscape. 

As a senior leader, you're expected to spearhead transformation efforts in response to these challenges.

In this programme, you will be equipped with practical frameworks and actionable strategies to lead and drive transformation effectively. Our approach synthesises current research and hands-on consultancy insights from working with senior leadership teams across various levels and layers in agencies. 

To make the most of this experience, we ask that you come prepared with
learning_outcomes:
Understand the crippling challenges and differentiating success factors of transformation
Explore the essential dimensions of strategy, leadership and culture, and what needs to be done differently to multiply the odds of success
Gain practical frames and actionable tools to apply and/or adapt for organisational transformation efforts
who_should_attend: You are a leader who is preparing for or in the midst of a divisional, departmental or organisational transformation

title: Foundation for Uplifting Service by Ron Kaufman
description:
(Do note that ELFUS13 was the previous code for this programme)

In our highly customised world, where customer experience is constantly evolving, people expect to be recognised and uniquely served. To stand out, your service must be Unbelievable! 

Uplifting Service provides service excellence workshops you need to continuously improve internal and external service performance. This suite of powerful tools enables the foundation of your superior and sustainable service culture.
learning_outcomes:
Learn a framework to continuously improve the customer service performance and employee engagement.
Build a common service language to help employees from different departments and levels communicate more quickly and easily.
Enable employees to evaluate processes and actions from the customer’s point of view and improve the customer journey– for external customers and internal colleagues.
Empower team members to earn compliments, resolve persistent customer complaints, and exceed customer expectations.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is keen in Service Excellence.

title: Masterclass in Gestalt Interventions
description:
This masterclass offers a holistic approach to working in large, complex social systems, with a strong emphasis on the core Gestalt concepts of use of self and personal presence. It includes lectures, experiential exercises, skills practice with feedback, case studies and analysis, reflection and journaling, and reading materials. Learning takes place in a range of settings, including individual work, pairs, groups, ad hoc structures, and plenary sessions.

Three internationally recognized Gestalt teachers and practitioners lead the programme.

Method of the Masterclass

Please note that there will be a pre and post class virtual session for this programme.

Pre-class Virtual Session: 20 July 2026, 8:00am - 10:00am
Physical Classroom: 3-6 August 2026, 9:00am - 5:00pm and 7 August 2026, 9:00am - 1:00pm
Post-class Virtual Session: 1 October 2026, 8:00am - 11:00am
learning_outcomes:
Experience and learn Gestalt theory and practice
Conceive of organisations as complex social systems existing within the context of a broader environment
Develop greater self-awareness and competence in use-of-self as an instrument of change and making a difference with your presence
Master the four primary models: Cycle of Experience, Unit of Work, Four Roles of the Gestalt Intervener, and the RSVP Fundamentals of Leadership Model
Recognise resistance to change and adopt a respectful and creative attitude toward resistance using the Gestalt Paradoxical Theory of Change
Enhance skills in attending to and intervening at multiple levels of system
who_should_attend:
You are an Organisation Development practitioner in the Singapore Public Service. You would like to develop your competence in adapting OD Interventions in your practice through designing and facilitating complex and unpredictable situations. Officers who also play an OD role in other functions are welcome. 

To qualify for entry to the Masterclass in Gestalt Interventions Programme, you will need to have attended either of these programmes: OD Graduate Certificate Programme (MSGCOD) / Intermediate Programme for OD Practitioners (MSIPOVL) / OD Practitioner Certification Programme (MSODCP). If you do not have these qualifications but believe that this programme is for you, please write in with reasons why.

title: Foundational Curriculum for Frontline Service Officers
description:
(Do note that CRFCFSO was the previous code for this programme)

Are you in your first year of frontline service work?

This curriculum equips you with the essential service skills you will need in your first year as a frontline service officer.

From serving as one Public Service using the CARE Service Principles to mastering empathetic communication, this curriculum will empower you to deliver effective services to citizens with confidence and resilience. Secure your spot today to upskill and become a confident frontline service officer.
