Please note that this is a blended curriculum comprising a series of curated e-learning programmes and two in-person training programmes. Access to e-learning programmes will be available within 2 weeks from your registration date until 70 days after the last day of your in-person class.

E-Learning Component

Learn at your own pace with our e-learning programmes:

First 100 Days: Onboarding for Service Officers
CARE Service Principles
Understandin
learning_outcomes:
By the end of the curriculum, you will be able to:

Articulate the role and responsibilities of frontline service officers.
Apply CARE service principles in interactions with citizens across various service touchpoints. 
Demonstrate personal resilience in managing change and difficult situations.
Apply empathetic communication techniques to manage difficult customer situations and enhance customer satisfaction.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is in the first year of frontline service.

title: Career Fitness for Mid-career Officers (Senior Officers)
description:
(Do note that CRCRE10 was the previous code for this programme)

In today's longer career journeys, experienced officers seek ways to reinvent their professional path with renewed purpose. This programme provides a structured approach to rediscover your career identity and transform your wealth of experience into meaningful opportunities. Through structured approaches and hands-on exercises, discover how to transform your wealth of experience into meaningful opportunities while managing work-life commitments to shape the second half of your next career chapter with renewed purpose and impact
learning_outcomes:
At the end of this programme, participants will be able to:

Discuss what career development is and why it is important
Rediscover personal values, interests and strengths to reinvent the second half of their career
Evaluate and leverage transferable skills and career interests to create new career possibilities
Explore career development resources and support to gather career insights
Recognise how structure and clarity lead to meaningful coaching conversations
Develop an actionable career plan with specific milestones and support mechanisms
who_should_attend: Public service officers (MX 13 and above)

title: Career Fitness for Mid-career Officers (Support Officers)
Driving Customer Experience (CX): An Organisational Systems Approach
Service Unwrapped: AI-gniting Service Innovation
description: ( Do note that CRSUSU1 was the previous code for this programme ) Service Unwrapped: AI-gniting Service Innovation takes you on a journey of discovery, unravelling the complexities of AI implementation layer by layer. This programme progressively unwraps practical insights and tools to help you harness GenAI for your service challenges. In this programme, you'll experiment with building a practical GenAI protoype for an actual service pain point you're facing, using accessible no-code platforms like Pair and AI Bots *.  We begin by helping you develop a clear problem statement, then gradually guide you through building your prototype solution. No technical background required. *Pair & AI Bots are accessible via GSIB
learning_outcomes: 1.    Describe the potential of GenAI in enhancing service delivery using examples from both public and private sectors to inspire innovative thinking in agency contexts 2.    Outline strategies and best-practices to overcome common GenAI adoption challenges in the service delivery context 3.    Identify service delivery workflow challenges that are well-suited for GenAI integration 4.    Prototype a simple GenAI service bot through hands on practice that can be implemented in their organisation with readily available tools (e.g. Pair, AI Bot)

title: Service Unwrapped: Insights to Impact
description: Service Unwrapped: Insights to Impact equips officers with the skills to turn customer data into action. This programme takes officers beyond technical data analysis, emphasising the journey from uncovering customer insights to engaging stakeholders for service improvements. Through hands-on practice, participants strengthen their sensemaking skills , synthesise findings into compelling problem statements , create prototypes that communicate ideas persuasively —enabling insight-driven decisions that improve service delivery and customer experience. Working on real workplace projects , participants will benefit from peer sharing and collaborative problem-solving. Participants will also have the opportunity to participate in a clinic session to share progress updates, receive feedback, and refine their approaches with support from fellow participants. By combining sensemaking skills with practical communication strategies, this programme empowers officers to bridge the gap between custom
learning_outcomes: Apply sensemaking methods to analyse customer data and uncover meaningful actionable insights.    Translate insights into well-defined problem statements.    Develop strategies to communicate customer insights persuasively to influence service decisions.

title: Service Values Workshop For Frontline Officers
description: ( Do note that CRSVW13 was the previous code for this programme ) Designed in partnership with ROHEI, the Service Values Workshop offers a multi-sensory, immersive learning experience. Through experiential learning activities, you'll learn to apply the Whole-of-Government CARE service principles, enabling you to deliver seamless and coordinated service to our citizens.   Note: This course is conducted at Rohei Campus (Devan Nair Institute, 80 Jurong East Street 21, #05-03)
learning_outcomes: Appreciate the significance of understanding the customers' perspective and putting them top of mind, displaying empathy and proactively resolving their issues, and placing people at the core in service delivery. Employ strategies to build and display emotional resilience, and learn to reframe and bounce back from difficult or challenging situations, while adopting a fresh perspective for delivering workplace excellence. Work collaboratively and effectively as part of a team within and across agencies and with multiple stakeholders, optimising for the greater good.

title: The ODExcelerator Programme for Advanced OD Practitioners
description:
Ready to elevate your OD practice and create system-wide impact? Sign up here.

Introducing the ODExcelerator Programme – a bespoke accelerator programme targeted at advanced OD practitioners – designed to deepen their competence, enhance their confidence and maximise system-wide impact while being deployed on OD consulting projects across different agencies or even at the Whole-of-Government levels.

The ODExcelerator Programme provides a structured, practice-driven pathway for advanced OD practitioners to deepen their consulting capability while progressing towards completion of the Executive Certificate for OD Professionals (ECOD) requirements. Through the programme, CSC-ILOD intentionally curates and plans practitioners’ consulting engagements, deploying them across appropriate OD projects and providing ongoing project supervision and mentoring throughout the journey.

By integrating practicum, supervision, and applied learning into a cohesive experience, the ODExcelerator enables
learning_outcomes:
1. Broadened Perspective & Knowledge: By working on consulting gigs outside of their organisation, OD practitioners will gain exposure to diverse organisational challenges and cultures. This broader perspective allows them to apply good practices from other agencies and contexts to solve problems in their own organisation, driving innovation and fresh solutions.

2. Enhanced Consulting Skills: The practicum allows practitioners to develop hands-on consulting skills, such as stakeholder management, diagnosis, intervention design, and facilitation. These skills will directly benefit the organisation, as practitioners bring back more refined expertise to manage internal OD projects, ensuring greater effectiveness and impact.

3. Professional Growth and Increased Confidence: This programme provides OD practitioners with the valuable opportunity to be mentored & supervised by senior OD practitioners with significant experience in both public and private sector contexts. As they grow in conf
who_should_attend:
You are an advanced-level Organisation Development practitioner with a strong passion in OD and a strong motivation to serve the Singapore Public Service and would like to develop your competence and confidence to support your leaders in achieving strategic outcomes and enhancing organisational health. 

ODExcelerator Prerequisites 

The ODExcelerator Programme is designed for advanced OD practitioners who are ready to deepen their consulting capability while progressing towards the completion of the Executive Certificate for OD Professionals (ECOD) requirements. The prerequisites are generally as follows: 

OD Practitioner Foundation Programme; AND
One of the following: Graduate Certificate in Organisation Development OR Intermediate OD Practitioner Programme (previous programme title) OR OD Practitioner Certification Programme (ODCP) OR Graduate Diploma in Leading and Sustaining Change (LSC). 

Selection Process:

Applicants who meet the programme prerequisites will be screened for e

title: Writing Empathetic Emails with GenAI
description: ( Do note that CREAI was the previous code for this programme ) Discover the power of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) in enhancing email communication with our upcoming 1-day programme.    The ‘Writing Empathetic Emails with GenAI’ (CREAI) programme is designed for public officers who are in service functions. It equips service officers with the knowledge and skills to leverage AI tools like Pair and SmartCompose to craft empathetic email responses to their customers. They will gain insights into the role and benefits of AI in email communication, and how to complement it with human connectedness and empathy.   Join us and harness the potential of AI in improving email structure, content, and tone, ensuring a seamless blend of technology and empathy in your correspondence with the public!   You are required to bring your GSIB laptop to class so that you can access to two AI tools easily, i.e. Pair and SmartCompose.   Please ensure that you have
learning_outcomes: By the end of this workshop, you will be able to:   Explain the role and benefits of AI in enhancing service delivery to customers via email communication within the public service.   Identify the limits of AI in understanding context and human emotions, reinforcing the irreplaceable value of human empathy.   Apply AI tools (Pair Chat and SmartCompose) to improve the structure, content, and empathetic tone of emails.  Draft responses to emails using AI while ensuring that issues are addressed with appropriate empathy.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Writing with Empathy
description: ( Do note that CRWEV10 was the previous code for this programme ) In the course of your work, you would have to write and respond to members of the public through emails. It is important that you foster positive and impactful interactions with the public through your writing. In this programme, you will learn to build trust and rapport with your audience by practising CARE and applying the key empathy principles. You will also gain a better understanding of their concerns and aspirations to write with empathy in your communications to the public. This programme is a virtual version of 'Engaging with Empathy: Writing'. There will be pre-programme as well as mid-programme assignments in between synchronous sessions.
learning_outcomes: Apply the CARE principles in your written communications Write to your intended audience with clarity (on purpose, message to be delivered and outcomes to achieve) Display empathy in your writing Adopt personalised and nuanced correspondences Discern issues that should or should not be addressed Adopt an appropriate approach when writing to the public

title: ComplianceConnect: Regulatory Tech
description:
Transform your regulatory practice with technology. This programme combines exposure to cutting-edge RegTech solutions at the MTX (Milipol TechX), an international public safety technology exhibition that brings together global innovators, technologists, and policymakers to advance transformative solutions, with hands-on training using government AI tools. Learn to identify AI-suitable regulatory challenges, prototype solutions using WOG tools like Pair and MS365 Copilot, and develop implementation strategies through our structured AI Impact Framework.

Join fellow practitioners in examining how technology-enabled, risk management approaches are shaping the future of regulatory excellence and gain valuable insights to drive your agency's compliance and enforcement transformation forward. Through interactive workshops, mini-hackathons, and peer learning, you'll gain practical skills to transform how your agency approaches compliance monitoring, investigation, enforcement, and regulatory
learning_outcomes:
Advocate the use of new technology to enhance regulatory practices and integrate them with existing processes.
 
Determine the applicability of emerging technologies to your agency's regulatory approach and evaluate feasibility (i.e. determine costs, benefits, and risks associated with new technology applications), test, scale and implement technology enabled solutions to improve regulatory efficiency and effectiveness.
 
Identify opportunities for regulatory innovation through stakeholder feedback, analyse trends to support adaptive regulatory strategies, and conduct After Action Review (AAR) on the implemented new technology to improve future application.
who_should_attend: This programme is open to all middle and senior level Regulatory, Compliance and Enforcement officers.

title: HR Analytics and Visualization: Drawing Insights From Data Analysis to Support Decision Making
Thinking and Writing Clearly for Policy Officers
description:
(Do note that CRFPP10 was the previous code for this programme)

This programme is designed for individual contributors in the Policy and Planning function.

It aims to sharpen essential skills of Deep Critical Thinking, and Clear and Concise Writing (under the Policy and Planning Competency framework), essential for excelling in policy work.  The programme includes a 2-hour self-paced eLearning module followed by an interactive 2-day classroom experience.

You will better appreciate, through interactive learning activities and case study discussions, the factors that lead to effective policy design, implementation, and evaluation. You will also learn how to communicate your policy intent, analysis, and recommendations concisely and clearly.
learning_outcomes:
At the end of the programme, P&P officers will be able to:

Define policy problems;
Analyse some common challenges in the policy process; and
Write and convey policies concisely and with clarity.
who_should_attend: New Policy and Planning (P&P) officers

title: Critical Thinking for Support Officers
description:
(Do note that CRFRCTS was the previous code for this programme)

This programme addresses the cognitive and emotional aspects of decision-making, helping learners identify and overcome their biases and emotional triggers. Through hands-on experience with AI tools for information gathering and analysis, combined with peer-learning sessions, learners would develop a comprehensive toolkit for enhanced critical thinking and decision-making in their professional roles. The practical approach also ensures immediate application of learned concepts to real-world challenges.
learning_outcomes:
Apply structured analytical frameworks to identify root causes and make sound judgement in various situations.  
Recognise and mitigate personal cognitive biases and emotional triggers that affect decision-making, while using models to enhance self-awareness and critical thinking capabilities.
Use AI to enhance information gathering and obtain diverse viewpoints to support the process of critical thinking.
who_should_attend: Support Officer

title: International Relations Starter Programme
description:
(Do note that CRIRSP1 was the previous code for this programme)

Designed for public officers new to international relations (IR), this two-day foundation course focuses on hands-on practice, with experienced IR practitioners guiding participants in developing essential diplomatic skills and knowledge of Singapore's approach to international work. The programme helps officers transition confidently into their IR roles, equipping them to navigate international engagements and represent Singapore's interests effectively..
learning_outcomes:
Understand the fundamental importance of international relations, Singapore's foreign policy framework and the role of public officers in advancing Singapore's international interests through the Whole-of-Government approach.
Apply essential diplomatic protocols, cultural sensitivities, and business etiquette required for effective engagement with international counterparts in various official settings, while keeping up our international relations interests.
Demonstrate practical skills in managing international work, including relationship building, negotiation strategies, and crisis management in diplomatic contexts.
who_should_attend: You are a public officer new to or recently assigned to roles involving international engagement across whole-of-government (less than three (3) years in an IR role). Officers must be Singaporean citizens to be eligible for this programme.

title: Ops-Tech Integration for Service Directors
description:
(Do note that CROTI10 was the previous code for this programme)

As a Service Director, are you increasingly looking for ways to innovate service delivery using technology? Implementing such innovations would require you to think about how to bridge operations and technical aspects, and to redesign your processes with considerations for technology affordances.

Join us in this programme to experience frameworks and tools that support closer collaborations between ops and tech teams to drive tech-enabled service innovation. Connect with industry experts, fellow Service Directors, and try your hand at solving real-life ops-tech integration issues!

This programme is organised in partnership with Singapore Management University, Executive Development (SMU-ExD), and supports the Skills for Digital Transformation (SDT) framework for Directors.
 

The programme is held at SMU's Campus. Details of the finalised location will be provided in LEARN DC upon placement.
learning_outcomes:
Discover opportunities for technology-enabled solutions in service operations
Explore methods to facilitate a user-centred and collaborative culture, to accelerate service delivery innovations effectively with technology partners
Understand the need for agility in balancing the user perspective with service provider capabilities and constraints in implementing technology-enabled innovations
who_should_attend: You are a Director or Deputy Director who leads the service or operations function in your organisation.

title: Spokesmanship - 2-day
description:
(Do note that CRSPM11 was the previous code for this programme)

 

Arm the media with well-crafted messages that are clear and concise, communicating your corporate messaging effectively. Understand how to engage the media from the perspective of a journalist. 

 

Media spokespersons need to be armed with the techniques, skills and knowledge in managing the media, regardless of the situation and the method of engagement. Strategic management of the media boosts brand and image of corporations. 

 

Deploying an effective training methodology of reinforcing knowledge and skills through hands-on practicum in a professional, realistic environment, this workshop is designed to impart participants with the tools, techniques, knowledge and skills in managing the media effectively, regardless of way the media engages them. 

 

This training is customised to suit the unique training needs of individual media spokespersons so that they will be able to engage the media looking good, sounding
learning_outcomes:
Gain insights to the basic dos and don’ts of virtual and in-person media engagement platforms
Be equipped with the essential skills of an effective media spokesperson such as techniques on facing the camera, screen presence, crafting bridging statements and key messages
Craft effective self-introductions
Plan and prepare how to face the media in the event of a crisis
Practice managing crisis communications through a camera simulation exercise
Gain tips and tricks on speech and presentation Delivery
Handle door-stop interviews
Manage media conferences/ press briefings
Gain experience through hands-on TV interviews and virtual interviews simulation exercises

title: OD Practitioner Foundation Programme
description: ( Do note that MSOD101 was the previous code for this programme ) Organisation Development (OD) is an increasingly influential field of practice in the Singapore Public Service - one that is employed to support organisational transformation, manage complex change, and enhance organisational performance, while ensuring that employees stay engaged in the process. If you have just joined the OD function and would like to learn more about how you, as an OD practitioner, can contribute to your organisation's health and effectiveness, this is the programme for you. In this updated version of the OD Practitioner Foundation Programme, you will gain an overview of the OD practice field, its history, and how it has evolved in the Singapore Public Service. You will undergo a unique, immersive experiential exercise, and learn a proven methodology that enables you to make sense of the complexities of organisational behaviour and the actions you can take to make a significant impact. The progra
learning_outcomes: Gain an overview of the OD practice field, its history and how it has evolved in the Singapore Public Service Develop insights into seeing organisations as systems, and some of the common and predictable challenges they face Appreciate how OD practice can contribute towards organisational health and performance Grow your network with the OD practitioner community in the Public Service

title: [Virtual Classroom] Design Play! for Support Officers
Multipliers: Bringing Out the Best in Your People
description:
(Do note that CRMULTS was the previous code for this programme)

Have you ever wondered why some leaders drain “intelligence” while others make the people around them “smarter”? Why do some leaders attract talent, while other leaders repel them? Now imagine if you could get at least 2x the intelligence and contribution from your teams. What could effective performance and results look like?

In today’s challenging landscape, leaders everywhere are facing increasing pressures to create more with less – to navigate uncertainty, lead change and build future-readiness in both their teams and themselves. We need all the intelligence and contribution from every team member to thrive and win.

Join us in this impactful workshop to master the Multipliers mindset and behaviors in real-life scenarios. Engage in simulations, discussions, activities, and experiments to uncover your multiplying and diminishing behaviors. Learn how to amplify the growth and success of your people and teams effective
learning_outcomes:
Discover the mindsets and practices that set Multiplier leaders apart from Diminisher leaders.
Enhance awareness of your behaviours that multiply or unintentionally diminish the potential of your people and teams.
who_should_attend: You are a Director or an experienced Deputy Director looking to multiply the potential of your people and teams to bring about better performance.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Wayfinding Career Fitness for your Team
description:
(Do note that CRWCTVL was the previous code for this programme)

Directors play an essential role in developing the careers of officers. In this webinar we will explore the role of Directors in building and sustaining an environment that enables and empowers officers to develop their careers. As a supervisor yourself, discover how you can have meaningful and impact career development conversations with officers.
learning_outcomes:
Discern your role as a leader to drive purposeful career conversations in your team that spark motivation, identifies growth opportunities, and brings clarity to development paths
Understand common career challenges and apply strategies to overcome them
Identify career development resources for the team, to create practical next steps

title: Managing Sousveillance in Enforcement
description: The proliferation and ubiquity of smart devices have forever changed the enforcement landscape. In particular, it has led to the rise of "sousveillance", where members of the public have no qualms recording audio and visual recordings of officers in action. Such recordings often find their way to social media, which places agency reputations at risk and imposes additional stress on the officers. In addition, there is a growing expectation for officers to fulfil dual functions of service and enforcement, which may not always be appreciated by the general public and can lead to misunderstandings being amplified by sousveillance. Officers need to thus keep themselves updated on the best ways to handle sousveillance situations in a way that focuses not only on deescalation, but also reduces the potential scope and reach on social media. Officers will also benefit from being made aware of healthy responses available to cope with the unique stressors resulting from by being the target of sou
learning_outcomes: Describe sousveillance and its impact on enforcement officers and agencies Describe a variety of constructive behavioural responses in a sousveillance situation Have increased self-awareness of their preferred behavioural responses when faced with a conflict and a wider range of options Identify the characteristics of a media issue and the impact on the organisation, and explain the role of enforcement officers in media communication Manage affected officer’s emotions and actions in response to social media crisis and sousveillance Recall effective communications strategies for officers responding to media/citizens/businesses on the ground and escalation of ground cases

title: [Virtual Classroom] E-Accessibility for an Inclusive Service Delivery
description: ( Do note that CREISVL was the previous code for this programme ) With increased digitalisation, more transactions, including public services, are moving online. However, persons with disabilities may find themselves unable to access important information or perform digital transactions if the content and platforms are not accessible to them.     In partnership with SG Enable, this programme introduces you to e-accessibility and the different types of assistive technology. You will gain a better understanding of the business case of making your digital content accessible. Through sharing of best practices, guidelines and experiential activities, learn the importance of e-accessibility and discover how you can make your agency’s content e-accessible.
learning_outcomes: Gain awareness of the business case for e-accessibility Understand the guidelines, codes and practices for e-accessibility Learn through hands-on experience with e-accessibility tools and practice developing a basic actionable plan

title: [Virtual Classroom] Mediation Skills for Public Officers
Understanding ASEAN
description: ( Do note that CRUNA10 was the previous code for this programme ) What is Singapore's relationship with ASEAN? How can Singapore safeguard and advance its national interests through ASEAN? In this programme, you will develop a deeper understanding of ASEAN, including its history, principles and structural frameworks. You will gain greater insight into the challenges and opportunities facing ASEAN, and how these relate to Singapore’s national interests. You will gain skills and knowledge to effectively engage with regional counterparts within ASEAN . You will also benefit from valuable insights from senior government officials and experienced ASEAN practitioners, who will share their expertise on understanding and navigating ASEAN.  About The Speakers Besides hearing from speakers from the S. Rajaratnam School of International Studies (RSIS), you will also hear from experienced diplomats like Mr Ong Keng Yong, currently Executive Deputy Chairman of RSIS, NTU and formerly ASEAN
learning_outcomes: • DESCRIBE Singapore’s interests in ASEAN  • EXPLAIN ASEAN’s history, principles and objectives, as well as mechanisms, and structures • EXPLAIN the roles of ASEAN’s various platforms in promoting cooperation among its member states and external partners • DISCUSS impacts of regional and international developments on Singapore and ASEAN *This programme will not cover the specifics of each country in Southeast Asia. Rather, it will focus on ASEAN, the regional platform.

title: Directors Leadership Series
description: ( Do note that MSDLS was the previous code for this programme ) Launched in November 2025, the Directors Leadership Series(DLS), a first of its kind in CSC’s programmes, is designed to support experienced Directors like yourself in your ongoing leadership and learning journey. The DLS is for those who have been in the Director role for several years in the Public Service and are thinking about what lies ahead for themselves and their leadership amidst the changing world.  You may sense a growing desire to carve out time and space from work to take stock of where one is. Perhaps something deeper is calling you to lead from a different place or to contribute in a different way? Perhaps meaning, impact and fulfillment should look different from here? If this resonates, the DLS may be the learning experience for you!
learning_outcomes: The DLS is a transformative learning series that offers you an exciting opportunity to Uplift your leadership competencies to meet the demands of this changing world Support you in discovering and creating a purposeful next chapter in your continued leadership journey Advocate a Public Service Leadership identity and cultivate a Director-level community. The DLS goes beyond a typical programme and focuses on leading from within,  community formation and leadership skills enhancement that applies across contexts and functions.

title: Communicating with Impact - A Clinic
description: From talking one-on-one to leading a Town Hall, we’re all expected to bring our best selves to the conversation – but some of these situations are more difficult than others. How do we inspire others instead of just stating facts or our opinions or ideas? How do we step into difficult conversations when we aren’t comfortable doing it or we don’t really know how to do it well? How do we facilitate sensitive conversations, disagreements and conflict between people? How do we develop our own leadership presence to support bringing our best selves into each conversation? This is a practical clinic for learning and practicing ways to communicate with greater impact.
learning_outcomes: Learn and practise the skills to be inspirational: develop a vision, transform it into a compelling message, and use storytelling to make it memorable Understand and practise handling difficult conversations, conflict and disagreements in a way that minimises negative feelings and impact Understand and practise connecting with oneself to create the foundation for leadership presence, creating a vision and staying centered even when others waver

title: [Virtual Classroom] Navigating Career Fitness for Your Staff
description:
(Do note that CRNCSVL was the previous code for this programme)

In this session, we will explore how managers can partner effectively in their officers' career development, creating value for both individuals and organisation. Through this session, supervisors will learn basic career coaching techniques to transform everyday workplace interactions into meaningful development conversations, and guide their staff to explore career aspirations, leverage their strengths, and set clear development goals.

title: Masterclass in 6 Conditions for Team Effectiveness
description: Ready to advance your OD practice and drive team effectiveness? Sign up here . This comprehensive two-module certification masterclass equips experienced OD practitioners with cutting-edge tools to diagnose and enhance team effectiveness across the public service. Built on the evidence-based 6 Team Conditions (6TC) model and Team Diagnostic Survey (TDS), you'll gain both foundational knowledge and advanced coaching capabilities to drive meaningful team transformation. Developed at Harvard University by world-renowned experts on teams and teaming – Dr. Richard Hackman, Dr. Ruth Wageman and Dr. Erin Lehman – the 6 Team Conditions and TDS predict up to 80% of a team's effectiveness. To maximise your learning impact, this advanced-level masterclass will be delivered through a series of mixed modalities. To kick-start your learning journey, you will gain access to the 6 Team Conditions e-learning portal, which introduces you to the science of team effectiveness and provides the conceptual f
learning_outcomes: Understand what team effectiveness is and how to measure it Learn about the 6 Conditions and how they influence behaviour, performance, and results in teams Know how to debrief a Team Diagnostic Survey (TDS) with team leaders and teams Learn how to put in place the 6 Conditions using a range of artful tools and techniques Work on cases to practise facilitation and coaching skills when debriefing TDS reports, designing team launches, and facilitating team coaching interventions Receive peer and instructor feedback to enhance your practice   Why This Masterclass? Evidence-based methodology proven in public sector contexts Progressive certification pathway from foundation to advanced practice Builds internal capability for sustainable team development Combines virtual accessibility with intensive face-to-face skill building Life-time access to a comprehensive advanced-level tools library of team effectiveness interventions    Trainers   Dr. Melissa J. Sayer, MBA Meliss

title: [Virtual Classroom] Managing Public with Mental Health Issues
description:
(Do note that CRMCMVL was the previous code for this programme)

Have you ever faced a member of the public (MoP) who was behaving strangely but you are not sure if it was because he/she had any underlying mental health issues and you were thus at a loss as to how you should handle the situation? Understand how best to handle MoPs with mental health issues, and participate in group discussions on scenarios and virtual role playing.
learning_outcomes: Identify, evaluate and manage various mental illnesses/disorders encountered in their workplaces
who_should_attend: You are a public officer in service operations functions and engaged in public-facing role.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Engaging Customers Professionally Face-to-Face
description:
(Do note that CRFCSVL was the previous code for this programme)

Despite the proliferation of technology and digitalisation, face-to-face interactions remain a critical touchpoint between public agencies and customers. It is through these interactions that connections are built, and trust established, with members of the public. Therefore, it is critical for us to have the skills to ensure each experience leaves a lasting, positive impression on our customers, and when faced with difficult situations, manage your emotions, so that you remain calm, non-judgemental and respond with thoughtful words and behaviours.
learning_outcomes:
Recall CARE Service Principles of the Singapore Public Service
Describe and apply quality service standards along the 4R process in face-to-face situations
Explain the connections between behaviour and impressions created
List ways to promote green service behaviour
Demonstrate positive vocal and visual skills when delivering service
Remain calm and manage behavioural responses to challenging service situations
Serve with firmness during difficult service encounters
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who interacts with external customers.

title: Graduate Certificate in Organisation Development
description: (Do note that MSGCOD was the previous code for this programme) Organisation Development (OD) capabilities are increasingly critical to sustaining organisational performance and health, especially as growth, disruptions and constant change accelerate. By helping organisations flourish through their people, OD builds transformative capacity and stronger organisations in volatile and complex environments. CSC’s OD milestone programmes are structured to enable dynamic, collaborative learning grounded in real and relatable Public Service contexts. They combine in-person classroom and self-directed learning to build both the depth and breadth of expertise required of an effective OD practitioner. Accredited by Civil Service College, the Graduate Certificate in Organisation Development Programme is designed with a strong focus on practical workplace application. It features a combination of plenary sessions, experiential components, small learning groups and assignments to broaden perspective
learning_outcomes: Build your capability and credibility as an Organisational Development (OD) practitioner by designing and delivering effective OD interventions that drive positive change within your organisation. Enhance your self-awareness and interpersonal skills to become a more effective OD practitioner by identifying and leveraging your strengths, addressing your developmental needs, and understanding the impact of your actions on others. Expand your knowledge of OD theories and frameworks and apply them to drive positive change in areas such as organisational change, team development, group dynamics, and stakeholder engagement. Apply a systemic approach to organisational diagnostics by using relevant examples and context from the public service to identify and address underlying issues that impact organisational effectiveness.  Design and implement system-wide change efforts using knowledge in organisation design, complexity and change.

title: Foundational Workshop for Enforcement Supervisors
description:
(Do note that CRFWS10 was the previous code for this programme)

Are you leading a team of frontline enforcement officer? What knowledge and mindset do you need to have in order to effectively lead your team? In this foundational programme, you will learn about governance, investigation and incident management theories, and be introduced to methods that will enable you to better lead frontline officers in performing their duties.
learning_outcomes:
Review enforcement implementation plans (including strategies, activities and resources) to optimise desired outcomes while maintaining a citizen-centric perspective
Create feedback loops between enforcement implementation and regulation with the aim of enhancing compliance
Ensure all evidence related to the case are appropriately obtained, documented, processed, and stored in accordance with agency policy and procedures to support case resolution
Assess and coach officers to respond professionally to difficult and exceptional incidents that disrupt daily operational activities (e.g. major threats to officer, agency, stakeholders and/ or general public)
Establish processes and systems, and adjust resources (e.g. manpower and assets, outsourced partners) to achieve enforcement outcomes
Coach officers to use appropriate interviewing techniques to obtain important information about the incident
Support officers to carry out case management processes with professionalism and integrity
who_should_attend: Supervisors who engage with members of public in regulatory, compliance, enforcement and operational job functions.

title: Foundational Workshop for Frontline Enforcement Officers
description:
(Do note that CRFWE13 was the previous code for this programme)

 

 

 

Join us at this one-day workshop to dive in-depth into the key concepts about the legal aspects of enforcement in Singapore, frontline incident management, investigative and crime scene management skills, and the basics of operation planning.

This introductory workshop aims to equip you with the necessary knowledge and skills to perform your duties well. Please complete the 1.5 hours e-learning on LEARN before coming for the workshop.
learning_outcomes:
Respond professionally to difficult and exceptional incidents that disrupt daily operational activities (e.g. major threats to officers, agency, stakeholders and/or the general public)
Plan and/or execute daily operations according to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) to optimise enforcement functions
Appreciate different privacy laws, security standards, and frameworks across countries and jurisdictions
Use appropriate interviewing techniques to obtain important information about the incident
Investigate potential enforcement violations (online and offline) by collecting and analysing evidence while considering the facts of the case
Carry out each step in the case management process with professionalism and integrity
who_should_attend: Public officers who engage with members of public in regulatory, compliance, enforcement and operational job functions.

title: Authentic Leadership Conversations for Managers
description:
(Do note that CRALCM was the previous code for this programme)

Managing difficult or uncomfortable discussions with direct reports, peers, or reporting officers can be a sensitive undertaking, especially when addressing delicate matters, diverse viewpoints, and intense emotions.


The Authentic Leadership Conversations for Officers programme provides conceptual models and concrete skills in carrying out authentic and impactful conversations that will help you address high-stakes conversations well.


It is a powerful, highly participative and practical programme that uses role-plays and real work situations commonly faced by learners. The programme will provide you with new insights and practical skills that you can apply immediately to tap into the power of conversations to transform the organisational culture for better engagement and alignment, translating to significant results.
learning_outcomes:
Improve the quality of conversation with all stakeholders - management, peers, team members, members of the public etc. 
Consciously build psychological safety with “point easy culture” so that all can speak openly and safely in a respectful manner.
Express opinions candidly on sensitive matters in a way that will not aggravate the situation 
Address difficult and sensitive situations with respect, authenticity and compassion.
Manage relationships to increase trust levels and improve relationship.
Identify potential problems or conflict situations early and undertake possible interventions.
Carry out difficult high stakes conversations with various stakeholders.
Take action to materialise matters discussed during conversations
who_should_attend: You are a manager who regularly engages in conversations with direct reports, peers, or reporting officers. It is particularly beneficial for those who need to address sensitive issues, manage diverse viewpoints, and navigate emotionally charged situations in their professional roles. It is also suitable for managers seeking to enhance their leadership communication skills, improve team dynamics, and foster a more engaged and aligned organisational culture.

title: 2-Day Protocol Workshop For Officers Sent On Liaison Duties
description: (Do note that CRPWLO2 was the previous code for this programme) As a liaison officer/planner, you will need to possess in-depth protocol skills and knowledge in order to carry out your responsibilities effectively. You will need to be aware of cross-cultural sensitivities, taboos and mannerisms, so as to respond and carry out your duties in a professional manner. Besides possessing fundamental protocol knowledge, you will also be required to understand and carry out specific protocol that goes into planning and handling an event, such as VIP management, liaison organisation and gift handling. This two-day programme aims to equip you with an in-depth training of protocol knowledge and skills needed for you to carry out your duties as a liaison officer/event planner. In this programme, you will be covering both the core subject of VIP handling, liaison and comms (item no.7) as well as a more specific understanding of VIP management (item no.9). The core subject of VIP handling covers the
learning_outcomes: Comprehend and practise principles of protocol and business etiquette, and be good minders to VVIPs and overseas guests at events and functions   Identify and demonstrate cross-cultural sensitivities and mannerisms towards guests   Extend appropriate courtesies in greeting, receiving, and sending off guests with correct formalities

title: 1-Day Protocol Workshop For Officers Sent On Liaison Duties
description:
(Do note that CRPWLO3 was the previous code for this programme)

As a new liaison officer, you will be required to possess fundamental protocol knowledge to carry out your responsibilities effectively. You will need to be aware of cultural sensitivities, taboos and mannerisms, so as to respond and carry out your duties in a professional manner.

This one-day programme is suitable for new liaison officers who wish to have a foundational protocol training, by introducing the key protocol skills needed for officers to exercise their liaison duties professionally.

Note: Please refer to this diagram illustrating the difference between the 1-day and 2-day Protocol Workshop For Officers Sent On Liaison Duties.
learning_outcomes:
Comprehend and practise principles of protocol and business etiquette, and be good minders to VVIPs and overseas guests at events and functions  
Identify and demonstrate cross-cultural sensitivities and mannerisms towards guests  
Extend appropriate courtesies in greeting, receiving, and sending off guests with correct formalities
who_should_attend: You have been or are likely to be entrusted with the responsibility to look after VVIPs and foreign guests. This workshop is open to Singapore Public Officers only.

title: Leading and transforming at scale: Building blueprints for impact
description:
(Do note that CRLTS was the previous code for this programme)

Successful transformations require strong sponsorship, engaging leadership, clear directions and concrete plans. Only then can a transformation lead to the intended substantive and systemic changes in our work and the way we work in our organisations. Hence, this programme - Leading and Transforming at Scale: Building Blueprints for Impact - will equip you with the practical knowledge, frameworks, tools and mindsets to lead your organisation’s transformation. The contents build on the foundation of our previous transformation programme and now integrate a systemic orientation, an organisation frame to scale up, and the transformation leadership role.

In the programme, you will build a blueprint to map the plans and shifts needed for your real-life transformation project. In addition, you will learn and apply concepts and templates for implementation along the different stages of transformation. You will also receive team c
learning_outcomes:
By the end of the programme, you will be able to effectively lead your current and future transformation efforts with the following:

Apply the CSC-ILOD transformation framework and approach to your organisation’s transformation project
Develop concrete blueprints for driving and managing transformation
Identify key actions for shifts and intervention
Engage your key sponsors to secure commitment to drive the transformation sustainably
who_should_attend: You are a leader or staff tasked with driving transformation or projects supporting your agency’s transformation. For maximum effectiveness of the programme, it is important for the project team leader (e.g. Transformation director) to attend the programme with key members of the change team.

title: Finance Development Programme
description:
(Do note that MSFDP was the previous code for this programme)

The Finance Development Programme (FDP) is a milestone programme jointly developed by MOF Finance and Procurement Academy (FPA) and Civil Service College (CSC) for junior finance officers who are starting out their careers in the public service.

This five-day programme will help you better understand your role in Public Service Finance and build peer networks within the Public Service Finance community. The programme is designed to bridge classroom learning with workplace application, supported by curated interventions that guide you in translating new insights into practice.

You are required to complete the Finance Foundation Programme (FFP) as a pre-requisite to this programme.
learning_outcomes:
By the end of the programme, participants will be able to:

Articulate  strategic  Whole-of-Government  priorities  and  align  them  with respective agency financial objectives
Explain how government finance components interconnect and influence government decision-making
Analyse the critical role of finance officers in driving transformation and providing strategic support
Apply essential finance knowledge in key Finance functions, operations and policy formulation required of a future government finance officer
Examine financial processes to identify improvement opportunities that align with organisational and government finance objectives
who_should_attend: By nomination only. This programme is designed for junior Public Finance officers with less than 3 years of working experience in the Public Service. Participants must have consistently good performance and be identified with potential to take on middle management roles by nominating agencies.

title: Data Cleaning & Visualisation for Support Officers
description:
(Do note that CRDCV10 was the previous code for this programme)

With the abundance of data at your fingertips, you may have spent many hours trying to clean up and make sense of untidy data. Your stakeholders may also have requested for you to better visualise and present data to support business recommendations. 

In this hands-on programme* supported by real-life case studies, you will use Microsoft Excel tools and gain foundational skills on how to clean and visualise data optimally and present a cohesive narrative that can influence business decisions. 

 

*This is an intermediate programme on data cleaning and visualisation for support officers only.
learning_outcomes:
Appreciate the role of data cleaning and visualisation in the analytics value chain 
Recognise the importance of structuring data in a tidy data format for data analysis and visualisation 
Appreciate key concepts and principles of data visualisation to generate reports 
Perform the steps of data cleaning and visualisation on a dataset using Microsoft Excel add-ins and tools 
Build interactive data reports using visualisation tools like Pivot tables 
Form a cohesive narrative and present data visually for actionable business insights
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer in a supporting role (MX13(I), MX14, MX15 and below) who is keen to strengthen your data analytics skills to clean and visualise data, and support the data work required to make better decisions.

title: The AIBots Lab
description:
Ready to evolve from curious observers into skilled bot architects? This comprehensive programme begins with participants developing problem statements that form the foundation of your AIBots solutions. You'll then engage in hands-on prototyping, finetuning advanced settings and features that elevate your AIBots performance.

Through collaborative troubleshooting sessions and peer-to-peer learning, you'll learn to optimise your bot whilst benefiting from diverse perspectives that improve bot functionality and user experience. By the end of this intensive one-day programme, you'll be equipped with the technical skills and confidence necessary to implement effective AIBot solutions within your agencies.

This is an 8-hour classroom programme (1 day) with a 2-hour pre-workshop eLearning component. The eLearning will be shared upon successful registration.

Learners would need to bring their own GSIB laptops with internet access for this programme. (note: SE-cards and S-GSIB machines are n
learning_outcomes:
By the end of this workshop, participants will be able to:  

Refine the problem statement for your AIBots use case
Prototype (or build) a functional bot using basic and relevant advanced features in AIBots
Troubleshoot and resolve bot issues using RAG assessment and job aids
Evaluate the bot's performance and make necessary revisions.
who_should_attend: This programme is open to all Singapore Public Officers seeking to develop AI bot solutions to improve their work processes.

title: RPA for Personal Productivity
description: ( Do note that CRRPAPP was the previous code for this programme ) Have you found yourself doing repetitive work that is manual, tedious and takes up more time than it should? Robotic Process Automation (RPA) can help you with this! While RPA is traditionally viewed as an area of expertise for tech officers, there is an increasing number of tools for business users to easily adopt RPA for routine tasks. “The most effective way to improve productivity is to eliminate work.” – William E. Conway, Jr. In this programme, explore suitable processes for automation and best practices for RPA implementation. There is also hands-on practice, where you can try your hand at building RPA bots for different use cases.
learning_outcomes: Appreciate the automation journey and how RPA can be used for personal productivity Identify suitable work processes for automation and whether process improvement is required Describe the RPA implementation approach and best practices Build RPA bots using UiPath StudioX and be comfortable in experimenting with the software’s functionalities Note: Laptops installed with the UiPath StudioX software will be provided for all participants during the programme.

title: Introduction to Python Programming
description:
(Do note that CRIPP10 was the previous code for this programme)

 

Python programming is a powerful one-stop tool that many are tapping on to not only solve complex data problems quickly and simply, but also enhance solutions. Thanks to Python’s low barriers to entry and versatility, even non-programmers can easily get a glimpse into the many possible fields where Python is able to help you maximise productivity.

Through hands-on exercises and resources in this programme, discover how to write simple programs that can assist you with data visualisation and developing automation for everyday tasks at work.
learning_outcomes:
Identify and explore automation using Python to improve process efficiency and service delivery.
Apply visualisation techniques using Python to convey insights and recommendations.
Execute basic descriptive analysis (mean, median, min, max, mode).
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who processes data and is keen to learn how to code with Python. You wish to learn how to programme daily tasks for automation and how to use a simple one-stop solution to visualise complex datasets. This programme is suitable for complete beginners with no prior programming knowledge. If you already have experience in another language, this programme will help you see the parallels between Python and the language that you already know. If you haven’t been programming in a while, this programme can be a good refresher of your dormant knowledge.

title: The Essence Of Lean Thinking
description:
(Do note that CRTET10 was the previous code for this programme)

In this current environment of increasing demand and limited resources, how can public agencies optimise resources and eliminate wastes to drive process and service improvements in the public sector?

Among process improvement methodologies, Lean is known for its simplicity to learn and apply in work processes. In this one-day programme, you will learn about Lean Thinking and its principles, particularly in reducing waste. There will also be hands-on opportunities to integrate knowledge of key Lean concepts, principles and tools.

Sign up for this workshop now to learn more about how Lean concepts and tools can be applicable in your work.
learning_outcomes:
Explain Lean thinking and its principles
Recognise and reduce wastes in processes
Identify common Lean tools and their applications
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer in a managerial role or in charge of process improvements. You are keen to learn more about Lean Thinking and its principles and tools.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Building your Career Mobility
description:
(Do note that CRBCMVL was the previous code for this programme)

Considering different career pathways has become increasingly common with individuals being more aware of their own career aspirations and actively seeking out opportunities to achieve their career goals. The Public Service also provides and encourages officers to take up opportunities such as secondments and structured job rotations (SJR), resulting in more officers experiencing changes in their careers.

While such experiences mark a new and exciting journey, we may also be apprehensive about how we can adapt to a new workplace culture and job scope, as well as building relationships with new colleagues.

Attend this 1-day virtual programme to learn how to apply the 4S Model of "Situation, Self, Support, Strategies" to identify resources and equip yourself with the right attitude and mindset to thrive in a new work environment, new colleagues and different work challenges.
learning_outcomes:
By the end of the programme, you will be able to:

Assess your current personal life stage, career interests and career experiences to identify short-term and long-term career priorities
Identify the support and resources needed for managing career changes
Develop a career transition action plan to manage upcoming career changes
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is keen to develop your mindset and competencies to be able to manage career transitions.

title: Introduction to Machine Learning Techniques (Code-free)
description:
(Do note that CRMLT10 was the previous code for this programme)

Organisations have started harnessing the power of their big data using modern technology like Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning, across sectors and job functions, e.g. Policy, Operations, HR, Finance or Procurement. This has led to better insights and productivity for the organisation. Take data science to the next level and learn the common machine learning techniques which may be used to help solve your agency’s business problems.

Through this programme, you will learn fundamental principles and techniques of machine learning. You will learn how to solve business problems using CRISP-framework by going through the entire cycle of modelling and testing for different use cases. You will understand how machine learning techniques can be used to predict behaviour, anticipate events, segment customers and even find outliers in your datasets.

You will also learn more about big data, and view demos of AI applicat
learning_outcomes:
Develop a basic understanding of the principles behind the algorithms of machine learning and CRISP-DM framework for data projects  
Learn and apply 4 main techniques in machine learning
Articulate use cases for the application of machine learning techniques to business problems within your agency.
Develop a basic understanding of deep learning, neural nets and big data, and the role they play in AI.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who wishes to learn more about the machine learning techniques and apply in an AI/ML project.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Driving Digital Excellence with The Six ‘I’s® - Unleash Your Innovation Potential
description: (Do note that CRAPIVL was the previous code for this programme) In the fast-paced world of public service, practical innovation is the driving force behind progress. As the government embraces digitalisation to elevate efficiency and effectiveness, public officers take the lead in shaping transformation. This programme is your gateway to the essential tools and mindsets of The Six ‘I’s® of Innovation – a structured, purpose-driven approach to turning ideas into value. Each participant receives a copy of Yes, You Can Innovate by Award winning author, Natalie Turner, and a personalised innovation strengths profile. As a public officer, your impact on the nation is profound. By unleashing your innovation potential, you hold the key to shaping a future of unparalleled public service excellence.
learning_outcomes: Structured Innovation: Embrace a clear and purposeful path to innovation, empowering you to tackle challenges with confidence. Harness Your Strengths: Discover your unique innovation abilities, amplify your skills, and learn how to refine them for lasting impact. The Six ‘I’s® Mindsets: Immerse yourself in the transformative power of The Six ‘I’s® – Curiosity, Creativity, Critical Thinking, Courage, Commitment, and Clever Thinking – igniting your capacity to innovate. Practical Tips and Tools: Gain hands-on, actionable tools within The Six ‘I’s® framework. Apply these techniques to your work in the Public Service for tangible and meaningful results.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Data Analytics – Basic Principles and Applications
description: (Do note that CRDDAVL was the previous code for this programme) With the abundant data and modern technology available today, there are rising expectations for non-analytics professionals to be proficient in data analysis to generate insights and improve decision making. Designed for non-analytics professionals, this foundational programme* will provide you with a good understanding and appreciation for data analysis, and how it can be used for better decision-making. Through practical hands-on exercises, analyse a given data set and solve case studies using Microsoft Excel tools such as pivot tables and pivot charts, and identify potential opportunities within your agency to apply data analysis. *This is a foundational programme that covers the whole process of data analytics using Excel, suitable for officers who wish to have an appreciation of data analytics.
learning_outcomes: Explain what data analytics is and the different types of data analytics - descriptive, predictive, prescriptive, quantitative and qualitative Describe the key questions that analytics can answer and how it can be used for better decision making Identify key tools and methodologies used in analytical decision making Apply basic data and statistical principles fundamental to data analytics Analyse data using pivot tables and basic tools in Microsoft Excel Apply principles of data visualisation Describe common techniques in predictive analytics

title: Love Them Or Lose Them: Motivating And Engaging Your Staff
description: (Do note that CRLEL02 was the previous code for this programme) Faced with a rapidly changing environment and citizens' increasing expectations, Public Service organisations need to respond by being more effective in their performance. To do so, organisations need to fully engage and retain their officers as they are the building blocks of the organisation. However, managers like you, with your multiple work responsibilities, may find it challenging to do so. This programme equips you with strategies, skills and practical tools to engage and retain your officers. It is specially designed for first-time line managers and is based on Wall Street bestseller, “Love 'Em or Lose 'Em: Getting Good People to Stay”.
learning_outcomes: Understand the causes and costs of disengagement Apply different strategies to effectively engage officers, ranging from millennial officers to longer serving officers Use practical tools to quickly understand the values and needs of different officers Know how you can use conversations to deepen trust and relationship with your officers Gain self-awareness about your strengths and weaknesses as a retention focused manager Conduct Stay Interviews with your officers instead of Exit Interviews

title: Data Analytics with R
description:
(Do note that CRDAR10 was the previous code for this programme)

With so many different programming languages available, which is the most beneficial for data analytics?

R is open source, making it highly cost-effective for a project of any size, and is also specifically designed for statistical analysis and data wrangling. In this programme*, discover how to program in R and use R for effective data analytics, through a range of practical hands-on activities and guided resources.

*This programme is suitable for officers in data analytics or data science fields who need to perform statistical analysis on large datasets.
learning_outcomes:
Identify and complete the range of tasks involved in the data analysis process
Appreciate R libraries and data structures using R
Analyse different datasets using R programming
Perform data cleaning for preparation of data analysis in R
Create data visualisations to communicate insights derived from datasets
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is keen to pick up R to do data analysis. You should have attended CSC's course Data Analytics – Basic Principles and Applications (CRDDA10/CRDDAVL) programme, or are familiar with basic data analytics. You should also be familiar with foundational statistical concepts.

title: Cleaning and Preparing Data for Analysis
description: (Do note that CRDCL10 was the previous code for this programme) Do you know that people often spend 80% of their time cleaning data and only about 20% doing the analysis? Good, clean data is not always readily available. The datasets we first encounter usually contain large volumes of data, stored in formats not easy to use, or contain inaccurate, incomplete or unreasonable data. Sometimes, there is also the need to combine data from different sources, which can be a tricky and messy job. In this programme*, you will use a range of hands-on activities and case studies to learn how to reverse the effort required so that you can spend your time analysing the data instead and improve your productivity. *This programme is suitable for officers who need to clean data at work.
learning_outcomes: Recognise the importance of structuring data in a tidy data format and applying the steps to convert ‘messy’ data to ‘tidy’ data for data analysis Describe the dimensions of data quality – validity, accuracy, completeness, consistency, uniformity – and demonstrate the ability to explore the data quality before starting data analysis Describe the data cleaning workflow of inspecting the data, cleaning the data and verifying the cleaning results Give examples of tools to use for data cleaning and preparation Demonstrate the ability to inspect a dataset to check the validity and profile the data using statistical summaries Perform the steps of data cleaning on a dataset using add-ins/ tools available in Microsoft Excel

title: Basic Data Analytics for Support Officers
description: (Do note that CRBDA10 was the previous code for this programme) As a support officer, you may have observed an increase in data collation requests to support decision making in key work processes or services. Would you like to unlock the insights behind the data you handle and see their impact? In this hands-on programme* supported by real-life case studies, you will use Microsoft Excel tools and gain foundational skills to analyse data and communicate insights to facilitate better decision-making. *This is a foundational programme on data analysis using Excel for support officers.
learning_outcomes: Explain what data analytics is and recognise different types of data analytics State the six key steps in analytical decision making Recognise basic data principles that form the foundation of data analytics Analyse data using pivot tables and basic tools available in Excel Produce Excel charts to analyse/ communicate data Describe the trends in analytics in both public and private sectors

title: Solving Problems With Lean
description: (Do note that CRSPL10 was the previous code for this programme) Are you keen to find out how public agencies can explore innovative ways to optimise limited resources and create more value for citizens? This activity-based programme is designed to help you develop skills and knowledge on solving workplace process-related problems. You will be required to identify an actual process related problem at work you wish to solve. Sign up now for a step-by-step guide through the Lean A3 thinking structured problem-solving approach by applying it on the problem you have identified.
learning_outcomes: Apply key Lean tools and techniques Solve an existing problem with the Lean A3 Problem Solving approach Apply the Lean A3 Problem Solving approach to other projects and challenges

title: Starting A Data Science Project
description:
(Do note that CRDSP10 was the previous code for this programme)

Have you been tasked to use data to solve a work-related problem but have no idea how to get started, where to collect data or how to analyse the data and present your insights?

Through this 3-day crash course* in Data Science for non-technical professionals, you will gain a fundamental understanding of various Data Science techniques by preparing and analysing multiple datasets using Python and performing actual data visualisation using Tableau. You will also improve your overall data literacy, and be guided through the entire Data Science process, from scoping a problem and data preparation to analysis and presentation of findings.

*This programme is suitable for officers who have a background in data analysis and statistics.
learning_outcomes:
Explain the impact of solving problems with data

Define a well-scoped problem statement, solvable through a Data Science approach

Perform simple cleansing of structured data

Identify where practitioners should be engaged for more complex work and collaborate effectively with them

Communicate insights and discoveries to stakeholders through clear narratives and visualisations
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer keen to adopt a data-driven approach to improving policy decisions and operations as well as to understand what is involved in starting a typical data science project.

title: Strategic Communications and Engagement for Policy Development
description:
(Do note that CRSCP10 was the previous code for this programme)

For government policies to be effective, the ability to craft and communicate persuasive messages tailored to diverse citizen segments is crucial.


This blended programme, which consists of a 4-hour self-paced eLearning module and a 1-day classroom programme, will help you understand the importance of strategic communications and engagement. It will explain the process of public communications in policy development, and will equip you with the skills to develop persuasive narratives to secure buy-in from diverse stakeholders.
learning_outcomes:
At the end of the programme, you will be able to:
Explain how strategic communications and public engagement strategies contribute to better policy development and implementation;
Craft customised narratives for different stakeholders in strategic communications and public engagement strategies; and
Design a strategic communication and public engagement strategy for a new policy deliverable.
who_should_attend: You are a Policy and Planning officer and middle manager involved in developing, implementing, and communicating policies.

title: Business Intelligence And Dashboarding
description: (Do note that CRAAD10 was the previous code for this programme) To support data-informed decision making, you need to be able to analyse data, generate insights and present them convincingly to your stakeholders. Elevate your data analysis, visualisation and communication today by using Business Intelligence (BI), the tool-driven process of analysing data, generating insights and presenting them. You will learn to use Microsoft’s Power BI tool to integrate, analyse data and create interactive dashboards through hands-on tasks. You will also learn how to communicate data-driven insights to stakeholders through a coherent and convincing story that has the power to influence decision-making.
learning_outcomes: Appreciate what business intelligence is and its role in analytics Describe how business intelligence and descriptive analysis can be applied to data Identify the importance of analysing data visually and selecting the right method of data analysis Analyse trends, spot outliers in data and find correlations Use visual perception principles to design insightful and effective dashboards Develop an interactive dashboard using Power BI to communicate insights coherently and convincingly

title: Advanced Presentation Strategies
description: ( Do note that CRAVP10 was the previous code for this programme ) Are you an experienced Singapore Public Officer who regularly delivers formal presentations to key stakeholders? This two-day programme is designed to enhance and refine your existing presentation skills by focusing on advanced techniques and strategies for impactful communication.  This programme is ideal for officers who already possess basic presentation skills and are looking to elevate their capabilities to effectively engage and influence their audience.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate the ability to analyse audiences using a structured four-step process, tailoring presentations for maximum impact. Exhibit the ability to prepare and deliver persuasive presentations, captivating and influencing audiences effectively. Acquire expertise in selecting visuals to effectively present and communicate data, guided by an understanding of visualisation objectives. Recognise the requirements of hybrid and virtual presentations for improved communication and engagement. Demonstrate the ability to present ideas effectively through storytelling. Gain advanced proficiency in managing Q&A sessions, including thinking on your feet and demonstrating verbal agility and poise when responding to audiences.

title: Presenting Evidence & Testifying In Court Trials
description: (Do note that CRPCT13 was the previous code for this programme) As an investigation officer, you may be required to attend court as an expert witness or to commence legal proceedings. In order to discharge your duties professionally, you need to know what is expected of you during the course of the legal proceedings in court. You will familiarise yourself with the legal and prosecution system of justice in Singapore. You will also learn to present evidence and testify in court trials confidently.
learning_outcomes: Examine the Singapore Legal System and administration of justice Demonstrate the skills required to present evidence and testify in Court confidently

title: Win-Win Mindset @ Work For Support Officers
description:
(Do note that CRDWM23 was the previous code for this programme)

When you have a win-win mindset, you will approach activities and relationships in the workplace in a positive manner and have a more productive and satisfying work-life. However, in order to inculcate such a mindset, you need to be able to manage the differences that exist among each another in order to bring about mutually beneficial outcomes to all involved. You can learn the necessary skills to bring about such a mindset.
learning_outcomes:
Illustrate some examples of win-win mindset at work
Use the Johari Window model to explain the benefits of self-disclosure
Identify different types of people and how they communicate
Use three approaches to relate with others for win-win in the workplace
Apply the Ladder of Inference and empathetic listening concepts in win-win conversations
Use different techniques to handle conflicts and resolve problems collaboratively
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who wishes to develop a win-win mindset to better support your team or department

title: Parliamentary Processes And Replies
description: (Do note that CRDPARL was the previous code for this programme) As a public officer, you may find yourself having to provide answers to questions posed to your ministry during parliamentary session. In this programme, you will learn how to draft effective parliamentary replies which emphasises your organisation's key messages to the public. You will also learn the business of Parliament and understand the PQ system, its procedures and timelines.
learning_outcomes: Understand the PQ system, its procedures and timelines. Draft replies that address the main concerns of the Members of Parliament (MPs), their constituents and the public, and emphasise the organisations key messages to the public

title: Writing Minutes Of Meetings For Support Officers
description:
(Do note that CRWRM23 was the previous code for this programme)

A good minute-taker is one who possesses a variety of skills. These include being able to summarise and listen effectively during a meeting; writing clearly, concisely and tactfully when translating notes into a set of minutes.

In this programme, you will learn and practise taking notes and writing minutes of meetings effectively and efficiently.
learning_outcomes:
Understand how meetings are conducted and the different ways of recording minutes and notes of discussions can be recorded clearly and concisely. 
Practise taking notes and writing minutes by listening and viewing the recording of some meetings
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who wants to write effective minutes of meeting to better support your team or department.
